definition: To leave completely and finally; to give up control of
exampleSentence: They had to abandon the project due to lack of funding.
exampleTranslation: 由于缺乏资金，他们不得不放弃该项目。

definition: To officially end a law, system, or institution
exampleSentence: The government voted to abolish the outdated regulation.
exampleTranslation: 政府投票废除了这项过时的法规。

definition: To take in or soak up energy, liquid, or information
exampleSentence: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
exampleTranslation: 植物从大气中吸收二氧化碳。

definition: Existing in thought or as an idea rather than physical
exampleSentence: The professor explained the abstract concept with simple examples.
exampleTranslation: 教授用简单的例子解释了这个抽象的概念。

definition: A very large quantity or amount of something
exampleSentence: The region is known for its abundance of natural resources.
exampleTranslation: 该地区以丰富的自然资源而闻名。

definition: To use something wrongly or to treat someone cruelly
exampleSentence: He was accused of abusing his authority in the office.
exampleTranslation: 他被指控滥用职权。

definition: A place of study or training in a specialized field
exampleSentence: She was admitted to the national academy of sciences.
exampleTranslation: 她被国家科学院录取了。

definition: To increase in speed or cause something to happen faster
exampleSentence: The company plans to accelerate its expansion into new markets.
exampleTranslation: 公司计划加速向新市场的扩张。

definition: The right or opportunity to use or approach something
exampleSentence: Students have free access to the university library resources.
exampleTranslation: 学生可以免费使用大学图书馆资源。

definition: To provide space or a place for someone to stay
exampleSentence: The hotel can accommodate up to three hundred guests.
exampleTranslation: 这家酒店可以容纳多达三百位客人。

definition: To succeed in doing or completing a task or goal
exampleSentence: She accomplished all her goals within the first year.
exampleTranslation: 她在第一年内完成了所有目标。

definition: To gather or collect gradually over a period of time
exampleSentence: Dust tends to accumulate on surfaces that are rarely cleaned.
exampleTranslation: 灰尘容易在很少清洁的表面上积聚。

definition: The quality of being correct, precise, and without error
exampleSentence: The accuracy of the data is essential for reliable research.
exampleTranslation: 数据的准确性对可靠的研究至关重要。

definition: Correct and exact in every detail without any errors
exampleSentence: The report provides an accurate description of the current situation.
exampleTranslation: 该报告准确描述了当前的情况。

definition: To say that someone has done something wrong or illegal
exampleSentence: The witness accused the defendant of committing the crime.
exampleTranslation: 证人指控被告犯了罪。

definition: To accept or admit the existence or truth of something
exampleSentence: The company acknowledged the mistake and issued an apology.
exampleTranslation: 公司承认了错误并发表了道歉。

definition: To make someone aware of or familiar with something
exampleSentence: Please acquaint yourself with the safety procedures before starting.
exampleTranslation: 请在开始之前熟悉安全程序。

definition: To obtain or gain possession of something over time
exampleSentence: The company acquired a smaller firm to expand its business.
exampleTranslation: 公司收购了一家小公司以扩展业务。

definition: To make something start working or become operative
exampleSentence: You need to activate your account before using the service.
exampleTranslation: 您需要在使用服务之前激活您的账户。

definition: Very serious or severe; having a sharp perception
exampleSentence: The patient was suffering from acute abdominal pain.
exampleTranslation: 患者正遭受急性腹痛的折磨。

definition: To change behavior or methods to suit new conditions
exampleSentence: Animals must adapt to their environment to survive.
exampleTranslation: 动物必须适应环境才能生存。

definition: Sufficient in quantity or quality for a particular purpose
exampleSentence: The school does not have adequate funding for new equipment.
exampleTranslation: 学校没有足够的资金购买新设备。

definition: To stick firmly to a surface or to follow rules closely
exampleSentence: All employees must adhere to the company code of conduct.
exampleTranslation: 所有员工必须遵守公司行为准则。

definition: Next to or very close to something without touching
exampleSentence: The park is adjacent to the main shopping district.
exampleTranslation: 公园毗邻主要商业区。

definition: To change slightly in order to make more suitable
exampleSentence: You may need to adjust the settings for better performance.
exampleTranslation: 您可能需要调整设置以获得更好的性能。

definition: To manage or be responsible for the operation of something
exampleSentence: The nurse will administer the medication to the patient.
exampleTranslation: 护士将给患者施用药物。

definition: To take up or start to use a new idea or method
exampleSentence: Many companies have adopted flexible working arrangements.
exampleTranslation: 许多公司已采纳了灵活的工作安排。

definition: The arrival or coming of an important event or invention
exampleSentence: The advent of the internet transformed global communication.
exampleTranslation: 互联网的出现改变了全球通信。

definition: Preventing success; harmful or unfavorable in effect
exampleSentence: The adverse weather conditions forced the cancellation of flights.
exampleTranslation: 恶劣的天气条件迫使航班取消。

definition: To publicly support or recommend a particular cause
exampleSentence: She advocates for equal rights in education for all children.
exampleTranslation: 她提倡所有儿童享有平等的教育权利。

definition: Existing or happening in the air or from aircraft
exampleSentence: The aerial photographs revealed ancient ruins hidden below.
exampleTranslation: 航拍照片揭示了隐藏在下方的古代遗迹。

definition: To officially join or become connected with an organization
exampleSentence: The local club decided to affiliate with the national organization.
exampleTranslation: 当地俱乐部决定隶属于全国性组织。

definition: To state or declare something positively and with confidence
exampleSentence: The court affirmed the lower court's decision on the case.
exampleTranslation: 法院维持了下级法院对该案的裁决。

definition: Having a great deal of money or wealth; prosperous
exampleSentence: The affluent neighborhood features large houses and private gardens.
exampleTranslation: 这个富裕的社区有大房子和私人花园。

definition: A list of items to be discussed at a meeting
exampleSentence: Climate change is at the top of the political agenda.
exampleTranslation: 气候变化是政治议程的首要问题。

definition: To make a problem or situation worse or more serious
exampleSentence: Smoking can aggravate existing respiratory health problems significantly.
exampleTranslation: 吸烟会显著加重已有的呼吸系统健康问题。

definition: Hostile or violent behavior or attitudes toward others
exampleSentence: The country condemned the act of military aggression strongly.
exampleTranslation: 该国强烈谴责了军事侵略行为。

definition: To make someone troubled or nervous; to campaign publicly
exampleSentence: The workers began to agitate for better wages and conditions.
exampleTranslation: 工人们开始鼓动争取更好的工资和条件。

definition: Quick to notice and respond to potential danger or problems
exampleSentence: Security guards must remain alert at all times during shifts.
exampleTranslation: 保安在值班期间必须始终保持警觉。

definition: Belonging to a foreign country; unfamiliar and strange
exampleSentence: The concept of individual freedom was alien to the culture.
exampleTranslation: 个人自由的概念对该文化来说是陌生的。

definition: To claim something as true without providing proof yet
exampleSentence: The report alleged that officials had accepted illegal payments.
exampleTranslation: 报告声称官员们收受了非法款项。

definition: A union formed for mutual benefit between groups or nations
exampleSentence: The two countries formed a strategic alliance against terrorism.
exampleTranslation: 两国结成了反恐战略联盟。

definition: To distribute resources or duties for a specific purpose
exampleSentence: The government will allocate more funds to public education.
exampleTranslation: 政府将拨出更多资金用于公共教育。

definition: To change in character or composition in a notable way
exampleSentence: The tailor can alter the dress to fit you perfectly.
exampleTranslation: 裁缝可以修改这件裙子使其完美合身。

definition: Occurring in turns or as every other one in a series
exampleSentence: The team works on alternate weekdays to maintain coverage.
exampleTranslation: 团队在交替的工作日上班以保持人员覆盖。

definition: The height of an object or point above sea level
exampleSentence: The plane was flying at an altitude of thirty thousand feet.
exampleTranslation: 飞机在三万英尺的高度飞行。

definition: A person who does something for pleasure rather than pay
exampleSentence: He is an amateur photographer who takes stunning landscape shots.
exampleTranslation: 他是一位拍摄出色风景照的业余摄影师。

definition: A diplomatic official representing their country abroad
exampleSentence: The ambassador presented her credentials to the foreign minister.
exampleTranslation: 大使向外交部长递交了国书。

definition: Having more than one possible meaning or interpretation
exampleSentence: The contract contained several ambiguous clauses that caused disputes.
exampleTranslation: 合同中有几个模棱两可的条款引起了争议。

definition: Having a strong desire and determination to succeed
exampleSentence: The government announced an ambitious plan for economic reform.
exampleTranslation: 政府宣布了一项雄心勃勃的经济改革计划。

definition: More than enough in size, scope, or capacity
exampleSentence: There is ample evidence to support the scientific theory.
exampleTranslation: 有充足的证据支持这一科学理论。

definition: A comparison between things that have similar features
exampleSentence: The teacher drew an analogy between the human brain and computers.
exampleTranslation: 老师将人脑与计算机进行了类比。

definition: To fix firmly in position; to provide stability for
exampleSentence: The new policy is anchored in principles of social justice.
exampleTranslation: 新政策以社会公正原则为基础。

definition: Not identified by name; of unknown identity or origin
exampleSentence: The donation was made by an anonymous benefactor last month.
exampleTranslation: 这笔捐款是上个月由一位匿名捐助者提供的。

definition: To expect or predict that something will happen soon
exampleSentence: Analysts anticipate a significant rise in oil prices this year.
exampleTranslation: 分析师预计今年油价将大幅上涨。

definition: A feeling of worry or unease about something uncertain
exampleSentence: Many students experience anxiety before important examinations begin.
exampleTranslation: 许多学生在重要考试开始前会感到焦虑。

definition: The equipment or machinery needed for a particular activity
exampleSentence: The laboratory is equipped with modern scientific apparatus.
exampleTranslation: 实验室配备了现代科学仪器。

definition: Clearly visible or understood; seeming real but not certain
exampleSentence: It was apparent that the system had a serious design flaw.
exampleTranslation: 很明显该系统有一个严重的设计缺陷。

definition: To make a serious or urgent request; to be attractive
exampleSentence: The charity appealed to the public for donations after the flood.
exampleTranslation: 慈善机构在洪水后向公众呼吁捐款。

definition: A natural desire for food or a strong wish for something
exampleSentence: The long hike gave everyone a healthy appetite for dinner.
exampleTranslation: 长途徒步让每个人都对晚餐有了好胃口。

definition: A device or machine designed for a specific household task
exampleSentence: Modern kitchen appliances have made cooking much more convenient.
exampleTranslation: 现代厨房电器使烹饪变得更加方便。

definition: To assess the value, quality, or nature of something
exampleSentence: The expert was hired to appraise the value of the antique.
exampleTranslation: 专家被聘请来评估这件古董的价值。

definition: Suitable or proper for the particular circumstances
exampleSentence: Wearing formal attire is appropriate for the business conference.
exampleTranslation: 穿正装出席商务会议是合适的。

definition: Close to the actual number or amount but not completely exact
exampleSentence: The approximate cost of the renovation is fifty thousand dollars.
exampleTranslation: 装修的大约费用是五万美元。

definition: Based on random choice rather than any reason or system
exampleSentence: The decision seemed completely arbitrary and lacked clear justification.
exampleTranslation: 这个决定似乎完全是武断的，缺乏明确的理由。

definition: An impressive display or collection of different things
exampleSentence: The museum has an impressive array of ancient artifacts on display.
exampleTranslation: 博物馆展示了大量令人印象深刻的古代文物。

definition: Having an exaggerated sense of one's own importance
exampleSentence: His arrogant attitude made it difficult to work with him.
exampleTranslation: 他傲慢的态度使得很难与他共事。

definition: To express thoughts or feelings clearly in words
exampleSentence: She was able to articulate her ideas with remarkable clarity.
exampleTranslation: 她能够非常清晰地表达自己的想法。

definition: To go up or climb to a higher position or level
exampleSentence: The hikers began to ascend the mountain at dawn.
exampleTranslation: 徒步旅行者在黎明时开始攀登这座山。

definition: To attribute something to a particular cause or source
exampleSentence: Many historians ascribe the fall of the empire to corruption.
exampleTranslation: 许多历史学家将帝国的衰落归因于腐败。

definition: To direct one's hopes toward achieving something great
exampleSentence: Many young people aspire to become successful entrepreneurs.
exampleTranslation: 许多年轻人渴望成为成功的企业家。

definition: A physical attack or a sudden violent military offensive
exampleSentence: The suspect was charged with assault after the street incident.
exampleTranslation: 嫌疑人在街头事件后被控袭击罪。

definition: To gather together in one place or to put parts together
exampleSentence: Workers assemble electronic components on the factory production line.
exampleTranslation: 工人们在工厂生产线上组装电子元件。

definition: To state a fact or belief confidently and forcefully
exampleSentence: The lawyer asserted that his client was completely innocent.
exampleTranslation: 律师断言他的当事人是完全无辜的。

definition: To evaluate or estimate the nature or quality of something
exampleSentence: The team was sent to assess the damage caused by the earthquake.
exampleTranslation: 团队被派去评估地震造成的损失。

definition: A useful or valuable thing, person, or quality possessed
exampleSentence: Her language skills are a valuable asset in international trade.
exampleTranslation: 她的语言技能是国际贸易中的宝贵资产。

definition: To allocate a task or responsibility to a specific person
exampleSentence: The manager will assign new tasks to each team member today.
exampleTranslation: 经理今天将给每个团队成员分配新任务。

definition: To absorb and integrate new ideas, knowledge, or culture
exampleSentence: Immigrants gradually assimilate into the culture of their new country.
exampleTranslation: 移民逐渐融入新国家的文化。

definition: To connect someone or something in one's mind with another
exampleSentence: Most people associate the color red with danger or warning.
exampleTranslation: 大多数人将红色与危险或警告联系在一起。

definition: To suppose something to be true without proof or evidence
exampleSentence: We cannot assume that the results will be the same.
exampleTranslation: 我们不能假定结果会是一样的。

definition: A positive declaration intended to give confidence or certainty
exampleSentence: The manager gave assurance that all employees would keep their jobs.
exampleTranslation: 经理保证所有员工都将保住工作。

definition: Protection granted to someone who has fled their own country
exampleSentence: Thousands of refugees applied for political asylum in the country.
exampleTranslation: 数千名难民在该国申请了政治庇护。

definition: The layer of gases surrounding earth or a pervading mood
exampleSentence: The restaurant has a warm and welcoming atmosphere for guests.
exampleTranslation: 这家餐厅有着温馨而热情的待客氛围。

definition: To succeed in achieving something after much effort
exampleSentence: She worked hard to attain her goal of becoming a doctor.
exampleTranslation: 她努力工作以实现成为医生的目标。

definition: To regard something as being caused by a particular factor
exampleSentence: Scientists attribute global warming to increased greenhouse gas emissions.
exampleTranslation: 科学家将全球变暖归因于温室气体排放增加。

definition: A public sale where goods are sold to the highest bidder
exampleSentence: The rare painting was sold at auction for two million dollars.
exampleTranslation: 这幅珍稀画作在拍卖会上以两百万美元售出。

definition: Genuine and real; not a copy or imitation of something
exampleSentence: The museum confirmed that the artifact is completely authentic.
exampleTranslation: 博物馆确认该文物完全是真品。

definition: To give official permission or approval for an action
exampleSentence: Only the director can authorize expenditures above ten thousand dollars.
exampleTranslation: 只有主管才能批准超过一万美元的支出。

definition: To convert a process to be operated by machines or computers
exampleSentence: The factory plans to automate its entire assembly line next year.
exampleTranslation: 工厂计划明年将整条装配线自动化。

definition: The right or condition of self-government and independence
exampleSentence: The university enjoys a high degree of academic autonomy.
exampleTranslation: 这所大学享有高度的学术自主权。

definition: To turn away or to prevent an undesirable event from happening
exampleSentence: Quick action by firefighters helped avert a major disaster.
exampleTranslation: 消防员的快速行动帮助避免了一场重大灾难。

definition: The flying or operating of aircraft as an industry
exampleSentence: The aviation industry has been severely impacted by rising fuel costs.
exampleTranslation: 航空业受到燃料成本上涨的严重影响。

definition: A person who holds a first university degree; unmarried man
exampleSentence: He earned a bachelor's degree in computer science last year.
exampleTranslation: 他去年获得了计算机科学学士学位。

definition: The chief support of a system or organization
exampleSentence: Small businesses form the backbone of the national economy.
exampleTranslation: 小企业构成了国民经济的支柱。

definition: A small piece of metal or cloth worn to show identity
exampleSentence: All visitors must wear an identification badge while on site.
exampleTranslation: 所有访客在现场期间必须佩戴身份徽章。

definition: To confuse or bewilder someone completely
exampleSentence: The mysterious disappearance continued to baffle the police investigators.
exampleTranslation: 神秘失踪事件继续困扰着警方调查人员。

definition: Declared legally unable to pay outstanding debts
exampleSentence: The company went bankrupt due to years of poor management.
exampleTranslation: 由于多年管理不善，该公司破产了。

definition: An agreement between parties on what each will do; a deal
exampleSentence: She found a great bargain at the annual clearance sale event.
exampleTranslation: 她在年度清仓特卖活动中找到了一件便宜货。

definition: Too poor in quality to produce crops or vegetation
exampleSentence: The barren landscape stretched for miles without any vegetation.
exampleTranslation: 贫瘠的土地绵延数英里，没有任何植被。

definition: A fence or obstacle that prevents movement or access
exampleSentence: Language can be a significant barrier to international communication.
exampleTranslation: 语言可能是国际交流的重大障碍。

definition: A group of things produced or dealt with at one time
exampleSentence: The factory produces a new batch of products every morning.
exampleTranslation: 工厂每天早上生产一批新产品。

definition: In the interest of or as a representative of someone
exampleSentence: The lawyer spoke on behalf of the defendant in court today.
exampleTranslation: 律师今天在法庭上代表被告发言。

definition: Resulting in good effects; favorable or advantageous
exampleSentence: Regular exercise is highly beneficial to both physical and mental health.
exampleTranslation: 定期锻炼对身心健康都非常有益。

definition: To be disloyal to or to reveal something unintentionally
exampleSentence: He would never betray the trust of his closest friends.
exampleTranslation: 他永远不会辜负最亲密朋友的信任。

definition: To cause someone to become confused and puzzled
exampleSentence: The complex instructions bewildered many of the new students.
exampleTranslation: 复杂的说明让许多新生感到困惑。

definition: An unfair prejudice in favor of or against something
exampleSentence: The researcher was careful to eliminate any bias from the study.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员小心翼翼地消除研究中的任何偏差。

definition: To mix or combine two or more substances together smoothly
exampleSentence: The chef knows how to blend spices to create unique flavors.
exampleTranslation: 厨师知道如何混合香料来创造独特的风味。

definition: A stupid or careless mistake with serious consequences
exampleSentence: The diplomatic blunder nearly caused a serious international incident.
exampleTranslation: 这一外交失误几乎引发了一场严重的国际事件。

definition: To talk with excessive pride about one's achievements
exampleSentence: He likes to boast about his achievements at every opportunity.
exampleTranslation: 他喜欢在每个机会吹嘘自己的成就。

definition: To support or strengthen something that is weakening
exampleSentence: The new evidence helped bolster the prosecution's case significantly.
exampleTranslation: 新证据大大加强了控方的案件。

definition: An extra payment or reward added to what is normally expected
exampleSentence: Employees received a generous year-end bonus for their hard work.
exampleTranslation: 员工因努力工作获得了丰厚的年终奖金。

definition: A period of rapid economic growth or sudden loud sound
exampleSentence: The technology boom created thousands of new jobs in the city.
exampleTranslation: 科技繁荣在该城市创造了数千个新工作岗位。

definition: To help or encourage something to increase or improve
exampleSentence: The marketing campaign helped boost sales by twenty percent.
exampleTranslation: 营销活动帮助将销售额提高了百分之二十。

definition: Certain or destined to happen; restricted or confined
exampleSentence: Problems are bound to occur when communication breaks down.
exampleTranslation: 当沟通中断时，问题一定会出现。

definition: A sudden important development or discovery in progress
exampleSentence: Scientists announced a major breakthrough in cancer treatment research.
exampleTranslation: 科学家宣布了癌症治疗研究的重大突破。

definition: To produce offspring or to cause a particular quality to develop
exampleSentence: Poverty and inequality can breed social unrest and conflict.
exampleTranslation: 贫困和不平等会滋生社会动荡和冲突。

definition: Money or gifts offered to influence someone's behavior illegally
exampleSentence: The official was arrested for accepting a bribe from contractors.
exampleTranslation: 该官员因收受承包商贿赂而被逮捕。

definition: Active and energetic; quick and busy in movement
exampleSentence: Business was brisk at the market during the holiday season.
exampleTranslation: 假日期间市场上的生意十分兴隆。

definition: Savagely violent or extremely harsh and unpleasant
exampleSentence: The documentary exposed the brutal conditions in the factory.
exampleTranslation: 这部纪录片揭露了工厂里残酷的工作条件。

definition: An estimate of income and expenditure for a set period
exampleSentence: The department needs to reduce its annual budget by ten percent.
exampleTranslation: 该部门需要将年度预算削减百分之十。

definition: The majority or greater part of something; large mass
exampleSentence: The bulk of the work was completed before the deadline.
exampleTranslation: 大部分工作在截止日期之前完成了。

definition: A heavy load; something oppressive that is carried
exampleSentence: The rising cost of living places a heavy burden on families.
exampleTranslation: 不断上涨的生活成本给家庭带来了沉重的负担。

definition: A system of government with complex rules and procedures
exampleSentence: Excessive bureaucracy often slows down the approval of new projects.
exampleTranslation: 过度的官僚主义往往会减缓新项目的审批速度。

definition: To go around or avoid an obstacle or a standard procedure
exampleSentence: The new highway will bypass the congested city center entirely.
exampleTranslation: 新公路将完全绕过拥堵的市中心。

definition: A body of senior government ministers or a storage cupboard
exampleSentence: The prime minister convened an emergency cabinet meeting immediately.
exampleTranslation: 首相立即召开了紧急内阁会议。

definition: An organized course of action to achieve a particular goal
exampleSentence: The company launched a new marketing campaign for its product.
exampleTranslation: 公司为其产品发起了新的营销活动。

definition: To decide that a planned event will not take place
exampleSentence: The airline was forced to cancel all flights due to fog.
exampleTranslation: 由于大雾，航空公司被迫取消了所有航班。

definition: A person who applies for a job or is nominated for election
exampleSentence: The company interviewed ten candidates for the management position.
exampleTranslation: 公司面试了十位管理职位的候选人。

definition: The power or ability to do something effectively
exampleSentence: The new software has the capability to process large datasets.
exampleTranslation: 新软件有能力处理大型数据集。

definition: A person who has been taken prisoner or confined
exampleSentence: The captives were held in a remote location for several weeks.
exampleTranslation: 俘虏被关押在一个偏远地点数周。

definition: Of the greatest importance; fundamental and essential
exampleSentence: Honesty is a cardinal virtue in any professional relationship.
exampleTranslation: 诚实是任何专业关系中的基本美德。

definition: A sudden event that causes great damage or suffering
exampleSentence: The oil spill was an environmental catastrophe of epic proportions.
exampleTranslation: 石油泄漏是一场史诗级的环境灾难。

definition: Care taken to avoid danger or mistakes; a warning
exampleSentence: Investors should exercise caution when entering volatile markets.
exampleTranslation: 投资者进入波动性市场时应谨慎行事。

definition: To come to an end or to stop doing something entirely
exampleSentence: The factory will cease operations at the end of this month.
exampleTranslation: 工厂将在本月底停止运营。

definition: To examine and suppress unacceptable parts of publications
exampleSentence: The government attempted to censor all media coverage of protests.
exampleTranslation: 政府试图审查所有关于抗议的媒体报道。

definition: An official count of a population and its characteristics
exampleSentence: The national census is conducted once every ten years.
exampleTranslation: 全国人口普查每十年进行一次。

definition: To officially confirm or attest something as true or genuine
exampleSentence: The document must be certified by a licensed notary public.
exampleTranslation: 该文件必须由持证公证人认证。

definition: Complete disorder and confusion in a situation
exampleSentence: The sudden power outage caused chaos in the busy airport.
exampleTranslation: 突然停电在繁忙的机场造成了混乱。

definition: To describe the distinctive qualities of someone or something
exampleSentence: Scientists characterize the disease by its rapid spread and symptoms.
exampleTranslation: 科学家以其快速传播和症状来描述该疾病的特征。

definition: A written statement of rights or principles; to hire transport
exampleSentence: The organization operates according to its founding charter principles.
exampleTranslation: 该组织按照其创始宪章的原则运作。

definition: To hold something dear and protect it lovingly
exampleSentence: She will always cherish the memories of her childhood summers.
exampleTranslation: 她将永远珍爱童年夏天的回忆。

definition: Persisting for a long time or constantly recurring
exampleSentence: The patient suffers from chronic back pain that limits mobility.
exampleTranslation: 患者患有限制行动的慢性背痛。

definition: The movement of something through a closed system or route
exampleSentence: The newspaper has a daily circulation of over one million copies.
exampleTranslation: 这份报纸的日发行量超过一百万份。

definition: To quote or refer to something as evidence or an example
exampleSentence: The researcher cited several studies to support her hypothesis.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员引用了多项研究来支持她的假设。

definition: Relating to a city or town and its citizens' activities
exampleSentence: Voting is an important civic duty in a democratic society.
exampleTranslation: 投票是民主社会中重要的公民义务。

definition: A person not in the armed forces or the police
exampleSentence: The conflict resulted in significant casualties among innocent civilians.
exampleTranslation: 冲突造成了无辜平民的重大伤亡。

definition: To make a statement or situation less confused and clearer
exampleSentence: Could you please clarify what you mean by that statement?
exampleTranslation: 你能澄清一下那个说法的意思吗？

definition: To come into conflict or be incompatible with something
exampleSentence: The two political factions clashed over the proposed tax reform.
exampleTranslation: 两个政治派别在拟议的税改问题上发生了冲突。

definition: A distinct section of a legal document or treaty
exampleSentence: The contract includes a clause about early termination penalties.
exampleTranslation: 合同包含一条关于提前终止罚金的条款。

definition: A person or organization using the services of a professional
exampleSentence: The law firm represents several high-profile corporate clients.
exampleTranslation: 该律师事务所代理了几位知名企业客户。

definition: The most intense or important point of something
exampleSentence: The story reaches its dramatic climax in the final chapter.
exampleTranslation: 故事在最后一章达到了戏剧性的高潮。

definition: To hold on tightly to something or to be reluctant to abandon
exampleSentence: Some people cling to traditional values in a rapidly changing world.
exampleTranslation: 一些人在快速变化的世界中坚持传统价值观。

definition: Relating to medical treatment or observation of patients
exampleSentence: The drug must pass clinical trials before public distribution.
exampleTranslation: 该药物在公开销售前必须通过临床试验。

definition: The act of closing something permanently; a feeling of resolution
exampleSentence: The factory closure resulted in hundreds of workers losing jobs.
exampleTranslation: 工厂的关闭导致数百名工人失业。

definition: A group of similar things growing or held closely together
exampleSentence: A cluster of stars was visible in the clear night sky.
exampleTranslation: 一簇星星在晴朗的夜空中清晰可见。

definition: A temporary alliance of groups for combined action
exampleSentence: The opposition parties formed a coalition to challenge the government.
exampleTranslation: 反对党组成了一个联盟来挑战政府。

definition: Relating to the mental processes of perception and reasoning
exampleSentence: The study examines the cognitive development of young children.
exampleTranslation: 该研究考察了幼儿的认知发展。

definition: Logical and consistent; forming a unified whole
exampleSentence: The student presented a coherent argument supported by strong evidence.
exampleTranslation: 学生提出了一个有充分证据支持的连贯论点。

definition: To occur at the same time or to be in agreement with
exampleSentence: The festival dates coincide with the start of the harvest season.
exampleTranslation: 节日日期与收获季节的开始相吻合。

definition: To work jointly with others on a shared activity or project
exampleSentence: The two universities will collaborate on a major research project.
exampleTranslation: 两所大学将合作开展一项重大研究项目。

definition: To fall down suddenly or to fail completely and suddenly
exampleSentence: The old bridge collapsed under the weight of heavy traffic.
exampleTranslation: 老桥在繁重交通的重压下倒塌了。

definition: To hit something violently while moving; to come into conflict
exampleSentence: The two vehicles collided at the intersection during rush hour.
exampleTranslation: 两辆车在高峰时段在十字路口相撞。

definition: Relating to or characteristic of a colony or colonialism
exampleSentence: The city's architecture reflects its colonial history and heritage.
exampleTranslation: 该城市的建筑反映了其殖民历史和遗产。

definition: To take action to reduce or prevent something undesirable
exampleSentence: The government introduced new measures to combat rising crime rates.
exampleTranslation: 政府出台了新措施来对抗不断上升的犯罪率。

definition: To begin or start something formally or officially
exampleSentence: Construction of the new hospital will commence next spring.
exampleTranslation: 新医院的建设将于明年春天开始。

definition: An expression of opinions about an event or situation
exampleSentence: The political commentary sparked a heated debate among viewers.
exampleTranslation: 这篇政治评论在观众中引发了激烈的辩论。

definition: The activity of buying and selling on a large scale
exampleSentence: International commerce has grown rapidly with the rise of technology.
exampleTranslation: 随着科技的发展，国际贸易迅速增长。

definition: An official group appointed to investigate or a fee for service
exampleSentence: The government established a commission to investigate the accident.
exampleTranslation: 政府成立了一个委员会来调查这起事故。

definition: A raw material or primary product that can be traded
exampleSentence: Oil is one of the most traded commodities in the world.
exampleTranslation: 石油是世界上交易量最大的商品之一。

definition: Not unusual; happening or found often and in many places
exampleSentence: Smartphones have become commonplace in modern daily life.
exampleTranslation: 智能手机在现代日常生活中已变得司空见惯。

definition: Shared by all members of a community; relating to a group
exampleSentence: The apartment building has a communal garden for all residents.
exampleTranslation: 公寓楼有一个供所有居民使用的公共花园。

definition: A person or animal with whom one spends time or travels
exampleSentence: The dog has been a loyal companion to the elderly woman.
exampleTranslation: 这只狗一直是这位老妇人忠实的伙伴。

definition: Of equivalent quality; able to be compared to another
exampleSentence: The two products are comparable in both quality and price.
exampleTranslation: 这两种产品在质量和价格上都具有可比性。

definition: A separate section or division of a structure or container
exampleSentence: The train has a first-class compartment with comfortable seating.
exampleTranslation: 火车有一个配有舒适座椅的头等车厢。

definition: Able to exist or work together without conflict
exampleSentence: The new software is compatible with most operating systems.
exampleTranslation: 新软件与大多数操作系统兼容。

definition: To force or oblige someone to do something against their will
exampleSentence: The evidence compelled the jury to reach a guilty verdict.
exampleTranslation: 证据迫使陪审团做出有罪裁决。

definition: To give something to reduce the bad effect of damage or loss
exampleSentence: The company agreed to compensate workers for their overtime hours.
exampleTranslation: 公司同意补偿工人的加班时间。

definition: Having sufficient skill or knowledge to do something well
exampleSentence: She is a highly competent manager who leads her team effectively.
exampleTranslation: 她是一位非常称职的经理，能有效地领导团队。

definition: To collect and assemble information from various sources
exampleSentence: The researchers compiled data from over fifty different studies.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员汇编了来自五十多项不同研究的数据。

definition: To add something that enhances or improves another thing
exampleSentence: The wine perfectly complements the flavors of the main dish.
exampleTranslation: 这款葡萄酒完美地补充了主菜的风味。

definition: The state of having many interconnected parts or factors
exampleSentence: The complexity of the problem requires a multidisciplinary approach.
exampleTranslation: 问题的复杂性需要多学科的方法来解决。

definition: To act in accordance with a wish, command, or rule
exampleSentence: All factories must comply with the new environmental regulations.
exampleTranslation: 所有工厂必须遵守新的环境法规。

definition: A part or element of a larger whole or system
exampleSentence: Each component of the machine must be inspected regularly.
exampleTranslation: 机器的每个组件都必须定期检查。

definition: Made up of various parts or elements combined together
exampleSentence: The bridge is built from a composite of steel and concrete.
exampleTranslation: 这座桥是由钢和混凝土的复合材料建造的。

definition: A substance formed by chemical combination of two or more elements
exampleSentence: Water is a chemical compound made of hydrogen and oxygen.
exampleTranslation: 水是由氢和氧组成的化合物。

definition: Including all or nearly all elements; thorough and complete
exampleSentence: The report provides a comprehensive analysis of the market trends.
exampleTranslation: 该报告对市场趋势进行了全面分析。

definition: An agreement reached by each side making concessions
exampleSentence: Both parties reached a compromise after lengthy negotiations.
exampleTranslation: 双方经过长时间谈判达成了妥协。

definition: Required by law or a rule; obligatory for everyone
exampleSentence: Education is compulsory for all children between ages six and sixteen.
exampleTranslation: 六岁至十六岁的所有儿童都必须接受义务教育。

definition: To form an idea or plan in the mind; to become pregnant
exampleSentence: It is hard to conceive of a world without modern technology.
exampleTranslation: 很难想象一个没有现代技术的世界。

definition: Something yielded or given up in negotiation or argument
exampleSentence: The union won several important concessions from the employer.
exampleTranslation: 工会从雇主那里赢得了几项重要的让步。

definition: To express strong disapproval of something as morally wrong
exampleSentence: World leaders condemned the terrorist attack in the strongest terms.
exampleTranslation: 世界各国领导人以最强烈的措辞谴责了恐怖袭击。

definition: To make denser or more concise; to change from gas to liquid
exampleSentence: She managed to condense the entire report into two pages.
exampleTranslation: 她设法将整个报告浓缩成两页。

definition: To admit that one has committed a crime or done something wrong
exampleSentence: The suspect finally confessed to the crime after hours of questioning.
exampleTranslation: 嫌疑人经过数小时的审讯终于承认了犯罪。

definition: To keep within certain limits or restrict to a specific place
exampleSentence: The discussion should be confined to the topic on the agenda.
exampleTranslation: 讨论应限于议程上的话题。

definition: To establish the truth or correctness of something previously
exampleSentence: The laboratory tests confirmed the initial diagnosis was correct.
exampleTranslation: 实验室测试证实了最初的诊断是正确的。

definition: To comply with rules or standards; to behave conventionally
exampleSentence: All products must conform to national safety standards before sale.
exampleTranslation: 所有产品在销售前必须符合国家安全标准。

definition: To face up to and deal with a difficult situation directly
exampleSentence: The country must confront the growing problem of climate change.
exampleTranslation: 国家必须面对日益严重的气候变化问题。

definition: To gather into a crowd or group in a particular place
exampleSentence: Students tend to congregate in the cafeteria between classes.
exampleTranslation: 学生们往往在课间聚集在食堂。

definition: A person's moral sense of right and wrong guiding behavior
exampleSentence: His conscience would not allow him to ignore the injustice.
exampleTranslation: 他的良心不允许他忽视这种不公正。

definition: Following each other continuously without interruption
exampleSentence: The team has won five consecutive matches this season.
exampleTranslation: 该队本赛季已经连续赢得了五场比赛。

definition: A general agreement among a group of people
exampleSentence: The committee reached a consensus on the new policy proposal.
exampleTranslation: 委员会就新政策提案达成了共识。

definition: Permission or agreement for something to happen
exampleSentence: Patients must give informed consent before undergoing any surgery.
exampleTranslation: 患者在接受任何手术前必须给予知情同意。

definition: The protection and preservation of natural resources
exampleSentence: Wildlife conservation is essential to maintaining global biodiversity.
exampleTranslation: 野生动物保护对维持全球生物多样性至关重要。

definition: Averse to change; holding traditional values and attitudes
exampleSentence: The bank takes a conservative approach to investment risk management.
exampleTranslation: 银行对投资风险管理采取保守的方法。

definition: Notably large in size, amount, or extent
exampleSentence: The project requires a considerable amount of time and resources.
exampleTranslation: 该项目需要相当多的时间和资源。

definition: To combine several things into a more effective whole
exampleSentence: The company plans to consolidate its three offices into one.
exampleTranslation: 公司计划将三个办公室合并为一个。

definition: Standing out so as to be clearly visible and noticeable
exampleSentence: The bright red car was conspicuous in the parking lot full of grey.
exampleTranslation: 那辆鲜红色的汽车在满是灰色车的停车场里非常显眼。

definition: A secret plan by a group to do something harmful or illegal
exampleSentence: The officials were charged with conspiracy to commit fraud.
exampleTranslation: 官员们被指控密谋实施欺诈。

definition: To be a component part of something or to form a whole
exampleSentence: Women constitute nearly half of the total workforce in the country.
exampleTranslation: 女性构成了全国劳动力总数的近一半。

definition: To severely restrict the scope or extent of something
exampleSentence: Budget cuts constrain the government's ability to fund public services.
exampleTranslation: 预算削减限制了政府资助公共服务的能力。

definition: To build or put together from parts or materials
exampleSentence: The workers will construct a new bridge over the river.
exampleTranslation: 工人们将在河上建造一座新桥。

definition: To seek information or advice from a person with expertise
exampleSentence: You should consult a doctor if the symptoms persist for days.
exampleTranslation: 如果症状持续数天，你应该咨询医生。

definition: To eat, drink, or use up a resource or commodity
exampleSentence: The factory consumes vast amounts of electricity every single month.
exampleTranslation: 工厂每个月消耗大量的电力。

definition: To make something impure by exposure to a harmful substance
exampleSentence: Industrial waste can contaminate rivers and underground water sources.
exampleTranslation: 工业废物会污染河流和地下水源。

definition: To think about something deeply and at length
exampleSentence: She sat quietly to contemplate the meaning of the poem.
exampleTranslation: 她静静地坐着思考这首诗的含义。

definition: To assert or maintain something as a position in argument
exampleSentence: Critics contend that the policy will harm small businesses.
exampleTranslation: 批评者认为该政策将损害小企业。

definition: A competition or event in which people try to win
exampleSentence: The singing contest attracted hundreds of talented participants.
exampleTranslation: 歌唱比赛吸引了数百名有才华的参赛者。

definition: The circumstances or setting in which something exists
exampleSentence: You must understand the historical context to interpret the document.
exampleTranslation: 你必须了解历史背景才能解读这份文件。

definition: To deny the truth of a statement by asserting the opposite
exampleSentence: The witness's testimony seemed to contradict earlier statements made.
exampleTranslation: 证人的证词似乎与之前的陈述相矛盾。

definition: Prolonged public disagreement or heated debate about something
exampleSentence: The new policy has sparked considerable controversy among citizens.
exampleTranslation: 新政策在市民中引发了相当大的争议。

definition: Based on what is traditionally done or believed; ordinary
exampleSentence: Conventional methods of farming are being replaced by modern techniques.
exampleTranslation: 传统的耕作方法正在被现代技术所取代。

definition: The process of changing from one form or state to another
exampleSentence: The conversion of the old factory into apartments is nearly complete.
exampleTranslation: 将旧工厂改建为公寓的工程即将完成。

definition: To communicate or make an idea or feeling known to others
exampleSentence: Words alone cannot convey the depth of gratitude we feel.
exampleTranslation: 仅凭言语无法传达我们感受到的感激之深。

definition: A firmly held belief or the finding of guilt in court
exampleSentence: She spoke with great conviction about the importance of education.
exampleTranslation: 她以极大的信念谈论了教育的重要性。

definition: To bring different elements into a harmonious relationship
exampleSentence: The manager will coordinate efforts between the two departments.
exampleTranslation: 经理将协调两个部门之间的工作。

definition: The exclusive legal right to reproduce a creative work
exampleSentence: The author holds the copyright to all her published novels.
exampleTranslation: 作者拥有她所有已出版小说的版权。

definition: To have a mutual relationship or connection between things
exampleSentence: Research shows that exercise levels correlate with mental wellbeing.
exampleTranslation: 研究表明运动水平与心理健康相关。

definition: Letters or communication exchanged between people
exampleSentence: The archives contain years of correspondence between the two leaders.
exampleTranslation: 档案中保存了两位领导人多年的通信。

definition: To destroy or damage metal gradually by chemical action
exampleSentence: Acid rain can corrode metal structures and stone buildings over time.
exampleTranslation: 酸雨会随着时间推移腐蚀金属结构和石建筑。

definition: Willing to act dishonestly for personal gain; morally depraved
exampleSentence: The corrupt official was sentenced to fifteen years in prison.
exampleTranslation: 腐败官员被判处十五年监禁。

definition: Advice given formally or a lawyer conducting a legal case
exampleSentence: The defendant has a right to legal counsel during the trial.
exampleTranslation: 被告在审判期间有权获得法律顾问。

definition: A person or thing that corresponds to another in function
exampleSentence: The minister met with her foreign counterpart to discuss trade.
exampleTranslation: 部长与外国对等官员会面讨论贸易问题。

definition: The showing of politeness and good manners toward others
exampleSentence: The staff treated every guest with courtesy and professionalism.
exampleTranslation: 工作人员以礼貌和专业精神对待每位客人。

definition: A person who is skilled in a particular craft or trade
exampleSentence: The furniture was made by a skilled craftsman using traditional methods.
exampleTranslation: 这件家具是由一位技艺高超的工匠用传统方法制作的。

definition: Able to be believed or trusted as honest and reliable
exampleSentence: The witness provided a credible account of the events that night.
exampleTranslation: 证人对那晚事件提供了可信的叙述。

definition: Standards by which something may be judged or decided
exampleSentence: Applicants must meet all the criteria listed in the job posting.
exampleTranslation: 申请人必须满足招聘启事中列出的所有标准。

definition: A detailed analysis and assessment of something; a review
exampleSentence: The professor gave a thorough critique of the student's thesis.
exampleTranslation: 教授对学生的论文进行了彻底的评论。

definition: Of great importance; decisive or critical to an outcome
exampleSentence: Timely medical intervention is crucial for the patient's recovery.
exampleTranslation: 及时的医疗干预对患者的康复至关重要。

definition: In a natural or raw state; lacking sophistication
exampleSentence: The country exports millions of barrels of crude oil annually.
exampleTranslation: 该国每年出口数百万桶原油。

definition: To press or squeeze with force so as to break or deform
exampleSentence: The machine can crush rocks into fine gravel for construction.
exampleTranslation: 这台机器能将岩石碎成细碎石用于建筑。

definition: A solid substance with a naturally geometric regular form
exampleSentence: The scientist studied the crystal structure of the mineral sample.
exampleTranslation: 科学家研究了矿物样本的晶体结构。

definition: To prepare land for crops or to develop a quality or skill
exampleSentence: Teachers should cultivate students' critical thinking abilities early.
exampleTranslation: 教师应尽早培养学生的批判性思维能力。

definition: Increasing by successive additions over a period of time
exampleSentence: The cumulative effect of the changes became evident over several years.
exampleTranslation: 这些变化的累积效应在几年后变得明显。

definition: Having skill in achieving one's ends by deceit or trickery
exampleSentence: The fox is often portrayed as a cunning animal in fables.
exampleTranslation: 狐狸在寓言中常被描绘为狡猾的动物。

definition: The subjects and content taught in a school or program
exampleSentence: The university updated its curriculum to include more digital courses.
exampleTranslation: 大学更新了课程设置，增加了更多数字化课程。

definition: The protective care or guardianship of someone or something
exampleSentence: The suspect was taken into police custody for further questioning.
exampleTranslation: 嫌疑人被警方拘留以作进一步讯问。

definition: According to the customs or usual practices of a group
exampleSentence: It is customary to shake hands when greeting someone formally.
exampleTranslation: 正式问候某人时握手是惯例。

definition: A solid or hollow geometric shape with straight parallel sides
exampleSentence: The engine has four cylinders that provide adequate power output.
exampleTranslation: 发动机有四个气缸，提供足够的动力输出。

definition: A structured set of data held in a computer system
exampleSentence: The company stores all customer information in a secure database.
exampleTranslation: 公司将所有客户信息存储在一个安全的数据库中。

definition: The latest time by which something must be completed
exampleSentence: The deadline for submitting the application is next Friday.
exampleTranslation: 提交申请的截止日期是下周五。

definition: Scattered fragments of waste or remains of something destroyed
exampleSentence: Rescue teams searched through the debris for trapped survivors.
exampleTranslation: 救援队在废墟中搜寻被困的幸存者。

definition: To rot or decompose through the action of natural processes
exampleSentence: The abandoned buildings had begun to decay from years of neglect.
exampleTranslation: 废弃的建筑因多年无人照管已开始腐朽。

definition: To cause someone to believe something that is not true
exampleSentence: The con artist managed to deceive hundreds of unsuspecting victims.
exampleTranslation: 骗子设法欺骗了数百名毫无戒心的受害者。

definition: Having the power to settle an issue; showing determination
exampleSentence: The general made a decisive move that turned the tide of battle.
exampleTranslation: 将军做出了扭转战局的决定性举动。

definition: A formal or explicit statement or announcement
exampleSentence: The declaration of independence was signed in seventeen seventy-six.
exampleTranslation: 独立宣言于一七七六年签署。

definition: To gradually become smaller, weaker, or less in quality
exampleSentence: The population of the rural village has declined steadily over decades.
exampleTranslation: 这个农村的人口在几十年里持续下降。

definition: An official order issued by a legal authority or ruler
exampleSentence: The emperor issued a decree banning all foreign trade immediately.
exampleTranslation: 皇帝颁布了一项立即禁止所有对外贸易的法令。

definition: To devote time or effort to a particular task or purpose
exampleSentence: She decided to dedicate her career to medical research.
exampleTranslation: 她决定将自己的职业生涯奉献给医学研究。

definition: A failure to fulfill an obligation; a preset standard setting
exampleSentence: The borrower was in default on the loan for three months.
exampleTranslation: 借款人已连续三个月拖欠贷款。

definition: A shortcoming or imperfection in something; a flaw
exampleSentence: The car was recalled due to a manufacturing defect in brakes.
exampleTranslation: 由于刹车的制造缺陷，该汽车被召回。

definition: A lack or shortage of something essential or required
exampleSentence: Vitamin D deficiency can lead to serious bone health problems.
exampleTranslation: 维生素D缺乏会导致严重的骨骼健康问题。

definition: The amount by which something falls short of requirement
exampleSentence: The government is struggling to reduce the national budget deficit.
exampleTranslation: 政府正努力减少国家预算赤字。

definition: Clearly stated or decided; not vague or doubtful
exampleSentence: There is no definite answer to this complex philosophical question.
exampleTranslation: 对于这个复杂的哲学问题没有明确的答案。

definition: To openly resist or refuse to obey rules or authority
exampleSentence: The activist chose to defy the unjust law through peaceful protest.
exampleTranslation: 活动家选择通过和平抗议违抗不公正的法律。

definition: To entrust a task or responsibility to another person
exampleSentence: A good leader knows when to delegate tasks to team members.
exampleTranslation: 好的领导知道何时将任务委派给团队成员。

definition: Done consciously and intentionally; careful and unhurried
exampleSentence: The attack was a deliberate act of sabotage against the facility.
exampleTranslation: 这次袭击是对该设施的蓄意破坏行为。

definition: Very fine in texture; requiring careful handling or sensitivity
exampleSentence: The negotiations are at a very delicate stage right now.
exampleTranslation: 谈判目前处于非常微妙的阶段。

definition: To clearly show the existence or truth of something
exampleSentence: The experiment was designed to demonstrate the effects of gravity.
exampleTranslation: 该实验旨在证明重力的影响。

definition: To publicly declare something to be wrong or evil
exampleSentence: Human rights groups denounced the government's treatment of prisoners.
exampleTranslation: 人权组织谴责了政府对囚犯的待遇。

definition: The degree of compactness of a substance or population
exampleSentence: The population density in urban areas is much higher than rural.
exampleTranslation: 城市地区的人口密度远高于农村。

definition: To leave a place, especially to start a journey elsewhere
exampleSentence: The train is scheduled to depart from the station at noon.
exampleTranslation: 火车预定中午从车站出发。

definition: A sum of money paid into a bank or as a first installment
exampleSentence: You need to pay a deposit of one month's rent in advance.
exampleTranslation: 你需要预付一个月的租金作为押金。

definition: To make someone feel very sad or to reduce economic activity
exampleSentence: The prolonged recession continued to depress the housing market.
exampleTranslation: 长期的经济衰退继续使房地产市场低迷。

definition: To prevent someone from having or using something essential
exampleSentence: The war deprived millions of children of access to basic education.
exampleTranslation: 战争剥夺了数百万儿童接受基础教育的机会。

definition: A person appointed to act on behalf of a senior official
exampleSentence: The deputy director will chair the meeting in her absence.
exampleTranslation: 副主任将在她缺席时主持会议。

definition: To obtain something from a specific source or origin
exampleSentence: Many English words derive from Latin and ancient Greek roots.
exampleTranslation: 许多英语单词源自拉丁语和古希腊语词根。

definition: To move downward or to be a blood relative of someone
exampleSentence: The hikers began to descend the mountain before sunset.
exampleTranslation: 徒步旅行者在日落前开始下山。

definition: To officially assign a particular status or name to something
exampleSentence: The area has been designated as a national wildlife sanctuary.
exampleTranslation: 该地区已被指定为国家野生动物保护区。

definition: Wished for as being attractive, useful, or necessary
exampleSentence: The apartment is in a highly desirable location near the park.
exampleTranslation: 这套公寓位于公园附近一个非常理想的位置。

definition: The complete loss of hope or expectation about something
exampleSentence: After months of searching without results she fell into despair.
exampleTranslation: 经过数月的无果搜索，她陷入了绝望。

definition: Feeling hopeless; having an urgent need or desire
exampleSentence: The desperate refugees walked for days to reach the safe border.
exampleTranslation: 绝望的难民步行数日到达安全边境。

definition: The events that will happen to someone in the future; fate
exampleSentence: She believed it was her destiny to become a great scientist.
exampleTranslation: 她相信成为伟大的科学家是她的命运。

definition: Causing great and irreparable harm or damage to something
exampleSentence: The earthquake was the most destructive natural disaster in decades.
exampleTranslation: 这次地震是几十年来破坏性最大的自然灾害。

definition: To separate or disconnect something from a larger body
exampleSentence: You can detach the keyboard from the tablet for easier use.
exampleTranslation: 你可以将键盘从平板电脑上拆下来方便使用。

definition: To discover or identify the presence of something hidden
exampleSentence: The sensor can detect even the smallest changes in temperature.
exampleTranslation: 传感器能检测到最微小的温度变化。

definition: The state of being kept in official custody or confinement
exampleSentence: The suspect was held in detention pending the outcome of trial.
exampleTranslation: 嫌疑人在等待审判结果期间被拘留。

definition: To become progressively worse in quality or condition
exampleSentence: The patient's health continued to deteriorate despite medical treatment.
exampleTranslation: 尽管接受了医疗治疗，患者的健康仍继续恶化。

definition: To depart from an established course or accepted standard
exampleSentence: The pilot was forced to deviate from the planned flight path.
exampleTranslation: 飞行员被迫偏离了计划的飞行路线。

definition: To plan or invent a procedure or mechanism by careful thought
exampleSentence: Engineers devised an innovative solution to the drainage problem.
exampleTranslation: 工程师们设计了一种创新的排水问题解决方案。

definition: To give time or resources to a particular activity or purpose
exampleSentence: He chose to devote his entire life to helping the poor.
exampleTranslation: 他选择将一生都奉献给帮助穷人。

definition: To identify the nature of an illness by examination
exampleSentence: The doctor was able to diagnose the rare disease quickly.
exampleTranslation: 医生能够迅速诊断出这种罕见的疾病。

definition: To break down food in the stomach or to absorb information
exampleSentence: It takes time to digest all the information from the lecture.
exampleTranslation: 消化讲座中的所有信息需要时间。

definition: The state of being worthy of honor and respect
exampleSentence: Every human being deserves to be treated with dignity and respect.
exampleTranslation: 每个人都应该受到有尊严和尊重的对待。

definition: A situation requiring a choice between equally undesirable options
exampleSentence: The company faces a dilemma between cutting costs and maintaining quality.
exampleTranslation: 公司面临着削减成本和维持质量之间的两难困境。

definition: A measurable extent of a particular kind such as length
exampleSentence: The social dimension of the problem is often overlooked by experts.
exampleTranslation: 问题的社会层面常常被专家忽视。

definition: To make or become less in size, importance, or value
exampleSentence: The heavy rain did not diminish the enthusiasm of the fans.
exampleTranslation: 大雨并没有减少球迷们的热情。

definition: A chemical compound containing two oxygen atoms per molecule
exampleSentence: Carbon dioxide emissions are a major cause of global warming.
exampleTranslation: 二氧化碳排放是全球变暖的主要原因。

definition: Relating to diplomacy or showing tact in dealing with people
exampleSentence: The president took a diplomatic approach to resolve the border dispute.
exampleTranslation: 总统采取外交方式解决边境争端。

definition: A book or website listing individuals or organizations
exampleSentence: You can find the contact details in the company staff directory.
exampleTranslation: 你可以在公司员工目录中找到联系方式。

definition: To make unable to function or to limit someone's movements
exampleSentence: The virus can disable the computer's security system completely.
exampleTranslation: 病毒可以完全使计算机的安全系统失效。

definition: To get rid of something as no longer useful or desirable
exampleSentence: Please discard any materials that are no longer needed for work.
exampleTranslation: 请丢弃任何工作中不再需要的材料。

definition: To release someone from duty or to emit a substance
exampleSentence: The factory was fined for illegally discharging waste into the river.
exampleTranslation: 该工厂因非法向河流排放废物而被罚款。

definition: To make previously secret information known to others
exampleSentence: The company refused to disclose the details of the financial deal.
exampleTranslation: 公司拒绝透露这笔金融交易的细节。

definition: Written or spoken communication or a formal discussion
exampleSentence: Academic discourse requires precise language and logical arguments.
exampleTranslation: 学术话语需要精确的语言和逻辑论证。

definition: Individually separate and distinct from one another
exampleSentence: The data can be divided into several discrete categories for analysis.
exampleTranslation: 数据可以分为几个离散的类别进行分析。

definition: Unjust treatment of different categories of people or things
exampleSentence: Racial discrimination in the workplace is strictly prohibited by law.
exampleTranslation: 法律严格禁止工作场所的种族歧视。

definition: To change appearance to prevent recognition by others
exampleSentence: The spy used a clever disguise to cross the enemy border.
exampleTranslation: 间谍用巧妙的伪装越过了敌方边境。

definition: To send away or to reject as unworthy of consideration
exampleSentence: The judge decided to dismiss the case due to lack of evidence.
exampleTranslation: 由于缺乏证据，法官决定驳回此案。

definition: A state of confusion or a medical condition affecting function
exampleSentence: The patient was diagnosed with a rare genetic blood disorder.
exampleTranslation: 患者被诊断出患有一种罕见的遗传性血液疾病。

definition: To send someone or something to a destination for a purpose
exampleSentence: The company dispatched a repair team to fix the equipment urgently.
exampleTranslation: 公司紧急派遣了一支维修队来修理设备。

definition: To move something from its usual place or position
exampleSentence: The flood displaced thousands of families from their homes.
exampleTranslation: 洪水迫使数千个家庭离开了家园。

definition: To get rid of something or to arrange things in order
exampleSentence: You should dispose of hazardous waste according to local regulations.
exampleTranslation: 你应该按照当地法规处理危险废物。

definition: A disagreement or argument about something important
exampleSentence: The land dispute between the two nations has lasted for decades.
exampleTranslation: 两国之间的领土争端已持续数十年。

definition: To interrupt the normal course or progress of an activity
exampleSentence: The protest threatened to disrupt traffic throughout the city center.
exampleTranslation: 抗议活动有可能扰乱整个市中心的交通。

definition: To cause a solid to become incorporated into a liquid
exampleSentence: Sugar dissolves quickly when stirred into warm water.
exampleTranslation: 糖在搅拌到温水中时会迅速溶解。

definition: Recognizably different from something else; clearly separate
exampleSentence: The two species are quite distinct in appearance and behavior.
exampleTranslation: 这两个物种在外观和行为上截然不同。

definition: To twist out of shape or to give a misleading account
exampleSentence: The media sometimes distort the facts to create sensational stories.
exampleTranslation: 媒体有时会歪曲事实来制造轰动性的报道。

definition: Extreme anxiety, sorrow, or pain; a state of danger
exampleSentence: The ship sent out a distress signal before it sank.
exampleTranslation: 船在沉没前发出了求救信号。

definition: To give shares of something to each of several recipients
exampleSentence: Volunteers helped distribute food and water to the flood victims.
exampleTranslation: 志愿者帮助向洪灾灾民分发食物和水。

definition: To interfere with the normal arrangement or functioning of
exampleSentence: Please do not disturb the other students during the examination.
exampleTranslation: 请不要在考试期间打扰其他学生。

definition: Showing a great deal of variety; very different from each other
exampleSentence: The city has a diverse population representing many different cultures.
exampleTranslation: 这座城市有着代表许多不同文化的多元化人口。

definition: A belief or set of beliefs held and taught by a group
exampleSentence: The political doctrine emphasized individual liberty above all else.
exampleTranslation: 这一政治学说将个人自由置于一切之上。

definition: An area of territory or a field of activity or knowledge
exampleSentence: Artificial intelligence is a rapidly expanding domain of research.
exampleTranslation: 人工智能是一个快速扩展的研究领域。

definition: Having power and influence over others; most prominent
exampleSentence: English has become the dominant language in international business.
exampleTranslation: 英语已成为国际商务中的主导语言。

definition: Something given to a charity or cause as a voluntary gift
exampleSentence: The hospital received a generous donation from a local philanthropist.
exampleTranslation: 医院收到了当地慈善家的慷慨捐赠。

definition: An unavoidable and usually adverse fate or terrible destiny
exampleSentence: The economic crisis seemed to seal the doom of many small businesses.
exampleTranslation: 经济危机似乎注定了许多小企业的命运。

definition: A quantity of medicine prescribed to be taken at one time
exampleSentence: The doctor recommended increasing the dose of the medication gradually.
exampleTranslation: 医生建议逐渐增加药物的剂量。

definition: A preliminary version of a piece of writing or a plan
exampleSentence: Please review the first draft of the report before the meeting.
exampleTranslation: 请在会议之前审阅报告的初稿。

definition: To cause liquid to flow away from something; to exhaust
exampleSentence: The constant workload began to drain his energy and motivation.
exampleTranslation: 持续的工作负荷开始耗尽他的精力和动力。

definition: Sudden and striking; relating to drama or the theater
exampleSentence: The company reported a dramatic increase in quarterly revenue.
exampleTranslation: 公司报告季度收入大幅增长。

definition: Likely to have a strong or far-reaching effect; extreme
exampleSentence: The government took drastic measures to control the spread of disease.
exampleTranslation: 政府采取了严厉措施来控制疾病传播。

definition: A feature that renders something less acceptable; a disadvantage
exampleSentence: The main drawback of the plan is its extremely high cost.
exampleTranslation: 该计划的主要缺点是其极高的成本。

definition: Consisting of two parts or having a double nature
exampleSentence: The device serves a dual purpose as both a phone and camera.
exampleTranslation: 该设备具有手机和相机的双重用途。

definition: To deposit or dispose of something carelessly or irresponsibly
exampleSentence: It is illegal to dump industrial waste in the river.
exampleTranslation: 向河流倾倒工业废物是违法的。

definition: Able to withstand wear, pressure, or damage over time
exampleSentence: The furniture is made from durable materials that last for years.
exampleTranslation: 家具由耐用材料制成，可以使用多年。

definition: The time during which something continues to exist or last
exampleSentence: Passengers must remain seated for the duration of the flight.
exampleTranslation: 乘客必须在飞行全程保持就座。

definition: To live in or at a specified place; to think about at length
exampleSentence: It is better to look forward than to dwell on past mistakes.
exampleTranslation: 向前看比纠结于过去的错误要好。

definition: Characterized by constant change, activity, or progress
exampleSentence: The city has a dynamic economy driven by technology and innovation.
exampleTranslation: 这座城市拥有以科技和创新驱动的充满活力的经济。

definition: Showing sincere and intense conviction about something
exampleSentence: She made an earnest appeal for peace and reconciliation.
exampleTranslation: 她真诚地呼吁和平与和解。

definition: A repeated sound caused by reflection of waves; a close parallel
exampleSentence: His words found an echo in the hearts of millions of listeners.
exampleTranslation: 他的话在数百万听众心中引起了共鸣。

definition: An obscuring of light from one celestial body by another
exampleSentence: The total solar eclipse attracted millions of viewers worldwide.
exampleTranslation: 日全食吸引了全世界数百万观众。

definition: The branch of biology studying organisms and their environment
exampleSentence: The study of ecology helps us understand environmental balance.
exampleTranslation: 生态学研究帮助我们了解环境平衡。

definition: A newspaper article giving the editor's opinion on a topic
exampleSentence: The editorial criticized the government's handling of the crisis.
exampleTranslation: 社论批评了政府对危机的处理方式。

definition: Involving many carefully arranged parts; detailed and complex
exampleSentence: The architect presented an elaborate plan for the new city center.
exampleTranslation: 建筑师为新的市中心提出了一份详尽的规划。

definition: Relating to or involving elections and the process of voting
exampleSentence: The electoral reform aims to make voting more accessible to all.
exampleTranslation: 选举改革旨在使所有人更容易参与投票。

definition: Graceful and stylish in appearance or manner
exampleSentence: She wore an elegant dress to the formal charity dinner event.
exampleTranslation: 她穿着一件优雅的礼服出席了慈善晚宴。

definition: To raise to a higher position or to a more important level
exampleSentence: The promotion will elevate her to the position of vice president.
exampleTranslation: 这次晋升将把她提升到副总裁的职位。

definition: Having the right to do or obtain something; satisfying conditions
exampleSentence: Citizens over eighteen are eligible to vote in national elections.
exampleTranslation: 十八岁以上的公民有资格参加全国选举投票。

definition: To completely remove or get rid of something unwanted
exampleSentence: The new system aims to eliminate errors in the production process.
exampleTranslation: 新系统旨在消除生产过程中的错误。

definition: Fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing
exampleSentence: The politician delivered an eloquent speech that moved the audience.
exampleTranslation: 政治家发表了一篇感人至深的雄辩演说。

definition: To fix an object firmly and deeply in a surrounding mass
exampleSentence: The journalist was embedded with the military unit during the conflict.
exampleTranslation: 这名记者在冲突期间被嵌入军事部队中。

definition: To give a body to a spirit or to represent an idea tangibly
exampleSentence: The new constitution embodies the principles of democracy and freedom.
exampleTranslation: 新宪法体现了民主和自由的原则。

definition: To hold someone closely or to accept something willingly
exampleSentence: The community was quick to embrace the innovative educational program.
exampleTranslation: 社区很快就接受了这个创新的教育项目。

definition: To move out of or come into view from a concealed position
exampleSentence: New evidence has emerged that could change the outcome of trial.
exampleTranslation: 新的证据已经出现，可能会改变审判结果。

definition: The production and discharge of gas or radiation into air
exampleSentence: Reducing carbon emissions is essential to combating climate change.
exampleTranslation: 减少碳排放对于应对气候变化至关重要。

definition: To produce and discharge something such as light or gas
exampleSentence: The factory emits large amounts of smoke into the atmosphere daily.
exampleTranslation: 工厂每天向大气中排放大量烟雾。

definition: Relating to or arousing intense feelings or strong reactions
exampleSentence: The farewell ceremony was an emotional event for all graduates.
exampleTranslation: 告别仪式对所有毕业生来说都是一次感人的活动。

definition: The supreme ruler of an empire with absolute authority
exampleSentence: The emperor ruled over a vast territory spanning three continents.
exampleTranslation: 皇帝统治着横跨三大洲的辽阔领土。

definition: Special importance or significance given to something
exampleSentence: The school places great emphasis on academic excellence and integrity.
exampleTranslation: 学校非常重视学业优秀和诚信。

definition: Based on observation or experience rather than theory alone
exampleSentence: The theory must be supported by empirical evidence from experiments.
exampleTranslation: 理论必须有来自实验的实证证据支持。

definition: To make a bill or proposal officially become part of law
exampleSentence: Congress voted to enact stricter regulations on air pollution.
exampleTranslation: 国会投票制定了更严格的空气污染法规。

definition: To surround or include a wide range of subjects or areas
exampleSentence: The curriculum encompasses subjects from science to the liberal arts.
exampleTranslation: 课程涵盖了从科学到文科的各个学科。

definition: To unexpectedly meet someone or experience a situation
exampleSentence: Travelers may encounter delays due to road construction this week.
exampleTranslation: 旅客本周可能会因道路施工而遇到延误。

definition: A serious determined effort to achieve a particular goal
exampleSentence: The space program represents humanity's greatest scientific endeavor.
exampleTranslation: 太空计划代表了人类最伟大的科学努力。

definition: To publicly declare approval or support for something
exampleSentence: The celebrity was paid to endorse the new line of products.
exampleTranslation: 这位名人被付费为新产品系列代言。

definition: To suffer through something painful without giving way
exampleSentence: The prisoners had to endure terrible conditions for many months.
exampleTranslation: 囚犯们不得不忍受数月的恶劣条件。

definition: To compel observance of a law, rule, or obligation
exampleSentence: The police will strictly enforce the new traffic safety regulations.
exampleTranslation: 警方将严格执行新的交通安全法规。

definition: A formal agreement to marry or active participation
exampleSentence: Community engagement is crucial for the success of local projects.
exampleTranslation: 社区参与对当地项目的成功至关重要。

definition: To intensify or improve the quality or value of something
exampleSentence: The new features are designed to enhance the user experience.
exampleTranslation: 新功能旨在增强用户体验。

definition: Very large in size, quantity, or extent; immense
exampleSentence: The project requires an enormous investment of time and money.
exampleTranslation: 该项目需要巨大的时间和金钱投资。

definition: To improve the quality or value of something significantly
exampleSentence: Studying abroad can greatly enrich your understanding of other cultures.
exampleTranslation: 出国留学可以极大地丰富你对其他文化的理解。

definition: To officially register as a member or participant
exampleSentence: Over five hundred students enrolled in the online course this semester.
exampleTranslation: 本学期有五百多名学生注册了这门网络课程。

definition: A business or company, especially a large or ambitious one
exampleSentence: The state encourages private enterprise to drive economic growth.
exampleTranslation: 国家鼓励私营企业推动经济增长。

definition: A thing with distinct and independent existence
exampleSentence: Each department operates as a separate entity within the organization.
exampleTranslation: 每个部门在组织内作为独立实体运作。

definition: A person who organizes and operates a business venture
exampleSentence: The young entrepreneur founded a successful technology startup.
exampleTranslation: 这位年轻的企业家创办了一家成功的科技初创公司。

definition: To contemplate or conceive of something as a future possibility
exampleSentence: It is hard to envisage a future without renewable energy sources.
exampleTranslation: 很难设想一个没有可再生能源的未来。

definition: A widespread occurrence of a disease in a community
exampleSentence: The government mobilized resources to contain the epidemic quickly.
exampleTranslation: 政府调动资源迅速控制了流行病。

definition: An event or period of time that is part of a larger series
exampleSentence: The diplomatic episode strained relations between the two countries.
exampleTranslation: 这一外交事件使两国关系紧张。

definition: The state of being equal in status, rights, and opportunities
exampleSentence: The movement fights for gender equality in all aspects of society.
exampleTranslation: 这一运动为社会各方面的性别平等而奋斗。

definition: To supply with the necessary items for a particular purpose
exampleSentence: The school is well equipped with modern teaching technology.
exampleTranslation: 学校配备了先进的教学技术。

definition: The quality of being fair and impartial; value of shares
exampleSentence: The government aims to promote social equity through education reform.
exampleTranslation: 政府旨在通过教育改革促进社会公平。

definition: Equal in value, function, or meaning to something else
exampleSentence: One dollar is roughly equivalent to seven yuan at current rates.
exampleTranslation: 按当前汇率，一美元大约等于七元人民币。

definition: To gradually wear away or destroy by natural forces
exampleSentence: Wind and rain continue to erode the ancient cliff face slowly.
exampleTranslation: 风雨继续缓慢地侵蚀着古老的悬崖面。

definition: To increase rapidly in intensity, extent, or severity
exampleSentence: The conflict could escalate into a full-scale war if not contained.
exampleTranslation: 如果不加以遏制，冲突可能升级为全面战争。

definition: The intrinsic nature or indispensable quality of something
exampleSentence: The essence of the argument is that education should be free.
exampleTranslation: 论点的本质是教育应该是免费的。

definition: Absolutely necessary and extremely important; fundamental
exampleSentence: Clean water is essential for human survival and good health.
exampleTranslation: 清洁的水对人类生存和健康至关重要。

definition: An extensive area of land or all property owned by someone
exampleSentence: The family owns a large estate in the countryside worth millions.
exampleTranslation: 这个家庭在乡下拥有一座价值数百万的大庄园。

definition: Respect and admiration for someone or something highly valued
exampleSentence: The professor is held in high esteem by students and colleagues.
exampleTranslation: 这位教授深受学生和同事的尊敬。

definition: Lasting or existing forever; without end or beginning
exampleSentence: The poem speaks of the eternal beauty of nature and love.
exampleTranslation: 这首诗讲述了自然和爱的永恒之美。

definition: A set of moral principles governing behavior or an activity
exampleSentence: Medical ethics requires doctors to prioritize patient welfare above all.
exampleTranslation: 医学伦理要求医生将患者福祉放在首位。

definition: Relating to a group sharing a common cultural tradition
exampleSentence: The region is home to many different ethnic groups and cultures.
exampleTranslation: 该地区是许多不同民族和文化的家园。

definition: To form an idea of the amount or value of something
exampleSentence: The committee will evaluate all proposals submitted by the deadline.
exampleTranslation: 委员会将评估截止日期前提交的所有提案。

definition: To turn from liquid into vapor or to disappear gradually
exampleSentence: The water will evaporate quickly under the hot summer sun.
exampleTranslation: 水在炎热的夏日阳光下会很快蒸发。

definition: Clearly seen or understood; obvious and plain to perceive
exampleSentence: It is evident that climate change is affecting weather patterns globally.
exampleTranslation: 很明显，气候变化正在影响全球的天气模式。

definition: To develop gradually over time through natural processes
exampleSentence: Technology continues to evolve at an unprecedented rate.
exampleTranslation: 技术继续以前所未有的速度发展。

definition: To represent something as larger or worse than it really is
exampleSentence: The media tends to exaggerate the severity of certain risks.
exampleTranslation: 媒体倾向于夸大某些风险的严重性。

definition: To be greater than a number or to go beyond what is allowed
exampleSentence: Sales this quarter exceeded all expectations set by the management.
exampleTranslation: 本季度的销售额超出了管理层设定的所有预期。

definition: To a very great extent; extremely or exceptionally
exampleSentence: The task proved to be exceedingly difficult for even expert analysts.
exampleTranslation: 这项任务即使对专家分析师来说也极其困难。

definition: Restricted to a particular person or group; not shared
exampleSentence: The hotel offers exclusive access to a private beach for guests.
exampleTranslation: 酒店为客人提供专属私人海滩通道。

definition: To carry out a plan or order; to put someone to death legally
exampleSentence: The team was able to execute the plan without any major problems.
exampleTranslation: 团队能够顺利执行该计划，没有出现重大问题。

definition: Free from an obligation that applies to others
exampleSentence: Non-profit organizations are exempt from paying certain taxes.
exampleTranslation: 非营利组织免缴某些税款。

definition: To apply a force, influence, or quality strongly or effectively
exampleSentence: Parents exert a powerful influence on their children's behavior.
exampleTranslation: 父母对孩子的行为施加着强大的影响。

definition: To drain someone of energy or to use up resources completely
exampleSentence: The long journey through the desert exhausted all of the travelers.
exampleTranslation: 穿越沙漠的长途旅行使所有旅行者筋疲力尽。

definition: To publicly display something or to show a quality clearly
exampleSentence: The museum will exhibit rare artifacts from ancient civilizations.
exampleTranslation: 博物馆将展出古代文明的珍贵文物。

definition: The state of being barred from one's native country
exampleSentence: The writer spent twenty years in exile before returning home.
exampleTranslation: 这位作家在流放中度过了二十年才回到家乡。

definition: Originating from a distant foreign country; strikingly unusual
exampleSentence: The garden features many exotic plants from tropical regions.
exampleTranslation: 花园里有许多来自热带地区的异国植物。

definition: A journey or voyage undertaken for a specific purpose
exampleSentence: The scientific expedition to Antarctica lasted for three months.
exampleTranslation: 南极科学考察持续了三个月。

definition: Expert skill or knowledge in a particular field or subject
exampleSentence: Her expertise in data science makes her invaluable to the team.
exampleTranslation: 她在数据科学方面的专长使她对团队非常宝贵。

definition: To come to an end or to cease to be valid after a time
exampleSentence: Your passport will expire in six months and needs renewal.
exampleTranslation: 你的护照将在六个月后到期，需要续签。

definition: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for doubt
exampleSentence: The contract contains explicit instructions about payment terms.
exampleTranslation: 合同包含关于付款条款的明确说明。

definition: To use something selfishly or to develop a resource fully
exampleSentence: The company was accused of exploiting workers in developing nations.
exampleTranslation: 该公司被指控剥削发展中国家的工人。

definition: A product sent to another country for sale; the act of sending
exampleSentence: China is one of the world's largest exporters of manufactured goods.
exampleTranslation: 中国是世界上最大的制成品出口国之一。

definition: To make something visible or to reveal hidden information
exampleSentence: The investigation exposed widespread corruption within the department.
exampleTranslation: 调查揭露了该部门内部的普遍腐败现象。

definition: The state of being exposed to something harmful or unpleasant
exampleSentence: Prolonged exposure to sunlight increases the risk of skin cancer.
exampleTranslation: 长时间暴露在阳光下会增加患皮肤癌的风险。

definition: Having no living members; no longer in existence
exampleSentence: Many species have become extinct due to habitat destruction.
exampleTranslation: 由于栖息地遭到破坏，许多物种已经灭绝。

definition: To remove or take out something by effort or force
exampleSentence: Scientists extract DNA from cells for use in genetic research.
exampleTranslation: 科学家从细胞中提取DNA用于基因研究。

definition: Very unusual or remarkable; beyond what is ordinary
exampleSentence: The athlete showed extraordinary talent from a very early age.
exampleTranslation: 这位运动员从很小的时候就表现出非凡的才能。

definition: Lacking restraint in spending; exceeding what is reasonable
exampleSentence: The wedding was an extravagant affair with over five hundred guests.
exampleTranslation: 婚礼非常奢华，有五百多位宾客出席。

definition: Reaching the highest degree; very severe or serious
exampleSentence: The region experiences extreme temperatures during the winter months.
exampleTranslation: 该地区在冬季会经历极端温度。

definition: To invent false information or to manufacture something
exampleSentence: The witness was caught trying to fabricate evidence for the trial.
exampleTranslation: 证人被发现试图为审判捏造证据。

definition: One side or aspect of something with many parts
exampleSentence: The report examines every facet of the company's business operations.
exampleTranslation: 报告考察了公司业务运营的每个方面。

definition: To make an action or process easier or more achievable
exampleSentence: The new software will facilitate communication between team members.
exampleTranslation: 新软件将促进团队成员之间的沟通。

definition: The teaching staff of a university or a natural ability
exampleSentence: The university faculty includes many internationally recognized scholars.
exampleTranslation: 大学教员中包括许多国际知名学者。

definition: Remaining loyal and steadfast in affection or allegiance
exampleSentence: The dog remained faithful to its owner throughout its entire life.
exampleTranslation: 这只狗一生都忠于它的主人。

definition: An extreme scarcity of food in a region causing widespread hunger
exampleSentence: The prolonged drought led to a devastating famine across the region.
exampleTranslation: 长期干旱导致了该地区毁灭性的饥荒。

definition: An imagined event or sequence of mental images; a genre
