exampleSentence: The novel is set in a fantasy world filled with magical creatures.
exampleTranslation: 这部小说设定在一个充满魔法生物的幻想世界中。

definition: To attract and hold the interest of someone irresistibly
exampleSentence: The documentary about deep-sea creatures fascinated viewers worldwide.
exampleTranslation: 关于深海生物的纪录片令全世界观众着迷。

definition: Causing death or having disastrous consequences
exampleSentence: The accident proved fatal for two of the passengers on board.
exampleTranslation: 这起事故对车上的两名乘客是致命的。

definition: Extreme tiredness resulting from mental or physical exertion
exampleSentence: Mental fatigue can impair decision-making ability and concentration.
exampleTranslation: 精神疲劳会损害决策能力和注意力。

definition: Expressing approval or to one's advantage; positive
exampleSentence: The market conditions are favorable for launching a new product now.
exampleTranslation: 目前的市场条件有利于推出新产品。

definition: Possible to do easily or conveniently; practical and workable
exampleSentence: The engineers confirmed that the construction plan is technically feasible.
exampleTranslation: 工程师确认该建设方案在技术上是可行的。

definition: An achievement requiring great courage, skill, or strength
exampleSentence: Landing on the moon was one of humanity's greatest engineering feats.
exampleTranslation: 登月是人类最伟大的工程壮举之一。

definition: Relating to a system of government with central authority
exampleSentence: Federal law takes precedence over state law in most legal cases.
exampleTranslation: 在大多数法律案件中，联邦法律优先于州法律。

definition: Information about performance used as a basis for improvement
exampleSentence: Customer feedback helps the company improve its products and services.
exampleTranslation: 客户反馈帮助公司改进其产品和服务。

definition: A friendly association or a grant for advanced study
exampleSentence: She was awarded a research fellowship at a prestigious university.
exampleTranslation: 她获得了一所著名大学的研究奖学金。

definition: Having qualities traditionally associated with women
exampleSentence: The brand markets itself with a modern and feminine aesthetic appeal.
exampleTranslation: 该品牌以现代和女性化的审美吸引力进行营销。

definition: The ability to produce offspring or the richness of soil
exampleSentence: The fertility of the soil in this region makes it ideal for farming.
exampleTranslation: 该地区土壤的肥沃使其非常适合耕种。

definition: Literature describing imaginary events and people; an invention
exampleSentence: The boundary between fact and fiction is sometimes hard to define.
exampleTranslation: 事实与虚构之间的界限有时很难界定。

definition: Having a violent or aggressive nature; very intense
exampleSentence: The two companies are locked in fierce competition for market share.
exampleTranslation: 两家公司在市场份额方面展开了激烈的竞争。

definition: To pass through a device to remove unwanted material
exampleSentence: The water must be filtered before it is safe for drinking.
exampleTranslation: 水必须经过过滤后才能安全饮用。

definition: Having limits or bounds; not infinite in size or extent
exampleSentence: The earth has only a finite supply of fossil fuel resources.
exampleTranslation: 地球的化石燃料资源供应是有限的。

definition: Relating to government revenue and public financial matters
exampleSentence: The government announced new fiscal policies to stimulate economic growth.
exampleTranslation: 政府宣布了新的财政政策以刺激经济增长。

definition: A piece of equipment permanently fixed in a position
exampleSentence: The bathroom fixtures were replaced during the building renovation.
exampleTranslation: 浴室固定装置在建筑翻新时被更换了。

definition: A mark or imperfection that mars something; a weakness
exampleSentence: The researcher discovered a fundamental flaw in the original theory.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员发现了原始理论中的一个根本缺陷。

definition: A group of ships or vehicles operating together under command
exampleSentence: The company manages a fleet of over two hundred delivery trucks.
exampleTranslation: 公司管理着一支超过两百辆送货卡车的车队。

definition: Capable of bending easily without breaking; adaptable
exampleSentence: The company offers flexible working hours to improve employee satisfaction.
exampleTranslation: 公司提供灵活的工作时间以提高员工满意度。

definition: A group of birds or animals; a large number of people
exampleSentence: Tourists flock to the beach resort during the summer holiday season.
exampleTranslation: 游客们在暑假期间涌向海滨度假胜地。

definition: To grow or develop in a healthy or vigorous way
exampleSentence: Small businesses tend to flourish in a supportive economic environment.
exampleTranslation: 小企业往往在支持性的经济环境中蓬勃发展。

definition: To rise and fall irregularly in number or amount
exampleSentence: Stock prices fluctuate daily based on supply and demand forces.
exampleTranslation: 股价每天根据供求关系波动。

definition: Relating to or located at the center of interest or activity
exampleSentence: Education reform has become the focal point of national policy debate.
exampleTranslation: 教育改革已成为国家政策辩论的焦点。

definition: To order someone not to do something; to prohibit by rule
exampleSentence: The law forbids the use of mobile phones while driving.
exampleTranslation: 法律禁止驾驶时使用手机。

definition: To predict or estimate a future event or trend
exampleSentence: Economists forecast moderate growth for the national economy next year.
exampleTranslation: 经济学家预测明年国民经济将适度增长。

definition: To create something strong or to produce a fraudulent copy
exampleSentence: The two nations worked to forge a lasting peace agreement.
exampleTranslation: 两国努力打造一项持久的和平协议。

definition: The way in which something is arranged or presented
exampleSentence: Please save the document in the correct file format before sending.
exampleTranslation: 请在发送前以正确的文件格式保存文档。

definition: A mathematical relationship expressed in symbols or a method
exampleSentence: The scientist developed a formula to calculate energy consumption.
exampleTranslation: 科学家开发了一个计算能耗的公式。

definition: To create or devise a strategy, plan, or set of rules
exampleSentence: The team needs to formulate a clear strategy for the project.
exampleTranslation: 团队需要为项目制定明确的策略。

definition: About to happen or appear; willing to give information
definitionZh: 即将来临的；乐于提供的
exampleSentence: Details about the forthcoming conference will be announced next week.
exampleTranslation: 即将举行的会议详情将于下周公布。

definition: A place or meeting for open discussion of public issues
exampleSentence: The international forum brought together experts from around the world.
exampleTranslation: 国际论坛汇集了来自世界各地的专家。

definition: The preserved remains of a prehistoric organism in rock
exampleSentence: The researchers discovered a rare dinosaur fossil in the desert.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员在沙漠中发现了一块稀有的恐龙化石。

definition: To encourage the development of something; to raise a child
exampleSentence: Good teachers foster a love of learning in their students.
exampleTranslation: 好老师培养学生对学习的热爱。

definition: A small part or amount of something; a numerical quantity
exampleSentence: Only a small fraction of applicants are accepted into the program.
exampleTranslation: 只有一小部分申请者被录取进入该项目。

definition: Easily broken or damaged; delicate and vulnerable
exampleSentence: The fragile ecosystem could be destroyed by further development.
exampleTranslation: 脆弱的生态系统可能会因进一步开发而被破坏。

definition: A small piece broken off from something larger; an incomplete part
exampleSentence: Archaeologists found fragments of ancient pottery at the excavation site.
exampleTranslation: 考古学家在发掘现场发现了古代陶器的碎片。

definition: A basic structure underlying a system or concept
exampleSentence: The government established a legal framework for environmental protection.
exampleTranslation: 政府建立了环境保护的法律框架。

definition: An authorization to sell a company's goods in a specific area
exampleSentence: The restaurant chain expanded through franchise agreements worldwide.
exampleTranslation: 这家连锁餐厅通过全球特许经营协议进行扩张。

definition: Wrongful deception intended for personal or financial gain
exampleSentence: The executive was convicted of financial fraud and sentenced to prison.
exampleTranslation: 这位高管因金融欺诈被定罪并被判入狱。

definition: Resistance when surfaces move against each other; conflict
exampleSentence: There is growing friction between the two departments over the budget.
exampleTranslation: 两个部门之间因预算问题产生了越来越多的摩擦。

definition: The outer edge or margin of something; not mainstream
exampleSentence: The idea was once considered fringe but is now widely accepted.
exampleTranslation: 这个想法曾经被认为是边缘性的，但现在已被广泛接受。

definition: To prevent someone from accomplishing a purpose; to discourage
exampleSentence: Bureaucratic delays continued to frustrate efforts to deliver aid.
exampleTranslation: 官僚主义的延误继续阻碍了援助的交付。

definition: To carry out or achieve something desired or promised
exampleSentence: The company must fulfil its contractual obligations to all clients.
exampleTranslation: 公司必须履行对所有客户的合同义务。

definition: Designed to be practical and useful rather than attractive
exampleSentence: The office furniture is simple but highly functional for daily use.
exampleTranslation: 办公家具简单但非常实用。

definition: Forming a necessary base or core; of central importance
exampleSentence: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right in democratic societies.
exampleTranslation: 言论自由是民主社会的一项基本权利。

definition: To provide a room with furniture or to supply with information
exampleSentence: The apartment was fully furnished with modern appliances and furniture.
exampleTranslation: 公寓配备了齐全的现代电器和家具。

definition: In addition to what has already been said; moreover
exampleSentence: The plan is too expensive; furthermore it is unlikely to succeed.
exampleTranslation: 该计划太昂贵了；此外，它不太可能成功。

definition: Wild and intense anger or violent and uncontrolled force
exampleSentence: The decision sparked fury among local residents and business owners.
exampleTranslation: 这一决定在当地居民和企业主中引发了愤怒。

definition: To join or blend together to form a single entity
exampleSentence: The artist's work fuses traditional techniques with modern digital art.
exampleTranslation: 这位艺术家的作品将传统技法与现代数字艺术融合。

definition: Incapable of producing any useful result; pointless
exampleSentence: All attempts to negotiate a peaceful settlement proved futile.
exampleTranslation: 所有谈判和平解决方案的尝试都证明是徒劳的。

definition: A system of millions of stars held together by gravitational force
exampleSentence: The Milky Way is just one of billions of galaxies in the universe.
exampleTranslation: 银河系只是宇宙中数十亿个星系之一。

definition: To take risky action in the hope of a desired result
exampleSentence: Investing all your money in one stock is a dangerous gamble.
exampleTranslation: 把所有钱投资在一只股票上是危险的赌博。

definition: Waste material or unwanted things discarded as useless
exampleSentence: The city has implemented a new garbage recycling program for residents.
exampleTranslation: 该市为居民实施了新的垃圾回收计划。

definition: An item of clothing worn on the body
exampleSentence: The factory produces high-quality garments for export to European markets.
exampleTranslation: 工厂生产高质量的服装出口到欧洲市场。

definition: To estimate or determine the amount or level of something
exampleSentence: It is difficult to gauge the full impact of the new policy.
exampleTranslation: 很难估计新政策的全部影响。

definition: Equipment or apparatus used for a particular purpose
exampleSentence: The hikers packed all the necessary gear for the mountain trip.
exampleTranslation: 徒步旅行者打包了登山旅行所需的所有装备。

definition: To make a general statement based on specific instances
exampleSentence: It is unwise to generalize about a culture based on limited experience.
exampleTranslation: 根据有限的经验来概括一种文化是不明智的。

definition: To cause something to exist or to produce energy or power
exampleSentence: Solar panels generate clean electricity from sunlight without pollution.
exampleTranslation: 太阳能电池板利用阳光发电，不产生污染。

definition: Exceptional intellectual or creative power or ability
exampleSentence: The mathematician was recognized as a genius from a very young age.
exampleTranslation: 这位数学家从很小的时候就被认为是天才。

definition: The deliberate killing of a large group of people
exampleSentence: The international court prosecuted those responsible for the genocide.
exampleTranslation: 国际法院起诉了那些对种族灭绝负责的人。

definition: A category of artistic composition characterized by style
exampleSentence: Science fiction is a popular literary genre among young readers.
exampleTranslation: 科幻小说是年轻读者中流行的文学类型。

definition: Truly what something is said to be; authentic and sincere
exampleSentence: Her genuine concern for the environment inspired many people.
exampleTranslation: 她对环境的真诚关心激励了许多人。

definition: A movement of the body to express an idea or meaning
exampleSentence: The president's visit was seen as a gesture of goodwill.
exampleTranslation: 总统的访问被视为一种善意的姿态。

definition: Extremely large or of very great size or extent
exampleSentence: The company invested a gigantic sum of money in new technology.
exampleTranslation: 公司在新技术上投入了巨额资金。

definition: Specified or stated; taking into account a particular fact
exampleSentence: Given the circumstances, the decision was entirely reasonable.
exampleTranslation: 考虑到当时的情况，这个决定是完全合理的。

definition: To stare angrily or to shine with a strong dazzling light
exampleSentence: The driver was blinded by the glare of the oncoming headlights.
exampleTranslation: 司机被迎面车灯的强光晃得睁不开眼。

definition: A momentary or partial view of something briefly seen
exampleSentence: The report offers a glimpse into the future of renewable energy.
exampleTranslation: 该报告让人得以一窥可再生能源的未来。

definition: Relating to the whole world; comprehensive in scope
exampleSentence: Climate change is a global issue that affects every nation equally.
exampleTranslation: 气候变化是一个影响每个国家的全球性问题。

definition: A state of partial or total darkness; a feeling of sadness
exampleSentence: A sense of gloom hung over the city after the tragic event.
exampleTranslation: 悲剧事件之后，城市笼罩着一种忧郁的气氛。

definition: High renown or honor won by notable achievements
exampleSentence: The team fought hard and earned glory by winning the championship.
exampleTranslation: 球队奋力拼搏，赢得了冠军的荣耀。

definition: Casual conversation about other people's private affairs
exampleSentence: Office gossip can damage relationships and reduce workplace morale.
exampleTranslation: 办公室八卦会损害人际关系并降低工作士气。

definition: To conduct the policy and affairs of a state or organization
exampleSentence: The new regulations will govern how companies handle personal data.
exampleTranslation: 新法规将管理公司处理个人数据的方式。

definition: Simple elegance or refinement of movement; courteous goodwill
exampleSentence: The ballet dancer moved across the stage with remarkable grace.
exampleTranslation: 芭蕾舞演员以非凡的优雅在舞台上移动。

definition: Taking place or progressing slowly over a period of time
exampleSentence: There has been a gradual improvement in the country's economic outlook.
exampleTranslation: 该国的经济前景逐渐好转。

definition: A piece of living tissue transplanted surgically; corruption
exampleSentence: The surgeon performed a skin graft to treat the severe burn injury.
exampleTranslation: 外科医生进行了皮肤移植来治疗严重的烧伤。

definition: A seed of a cereal plant; a very small amount of something
exampleSentence: The country imports millions of tons of grain annually.
exampleTranslation: 该国每年进口数百万吨粮食。

definition: To give or allow something requested; a sum of money given
exampleSentence: The university received a research grant from the federal government.
exampleTranslation: 这所大学获得了联邦政府的研究拨款。

definition: Relating to visual art or giving vivid detail about something
exampleSentence: The documentary contained graphic images of the war's devastation.
exampleTranslation: 这部纪录片包含了战争破坏的生动画面。

definition: To seize and hold firmly or to understand something completely
exampleSentence: Students need time to fully grasp the complex mathematical concepts.
exampleTranslation: 学生需要时间来完全理解复杂的数学概念。

definition: Feeling or showing thanks for kindness received from others
exampleSentence: We are deeply grateful for your generous support and contributions.
exampleTranslation: 我们对您慷慨的支持和贡献深表感谢。

definition: Giving cause for alarm; very serious and important
exampleSentence: The doctor expressed grave concern about the patient's worsening condition.
exampleTranslation: 医生对患者恶化的病情表示了严重关切。

definition: The force that attracts objects toward earth; seriousness
exampleSentence: The gravity of the situation required immediate action from leaders.
exampleTranslation: 局势的严重性要求领导人立即采取行动。

definition: Intense sorrow especially caused by someone's death
exampleSentence: The entire community was united in grief after the tragic accident.
exampleTranslation: 整个社区在悲惨事故后团结在悲痛之中。

definition: Very serious or gloomy; depressing or worrying to consider
exampleSentence: The economic forecast paints a grim picture for the coming year.
exampleTranslation: 经济预测描绘了来年严峻的前景。

definition: To hold very tightly or to have a powerful effect on someone
exampleSentence: Fear gripped the entire nation during the prolonged crisis.
exampleTranslation: 在长期危机期间，恐惧笼罩了整个国家。

definition: Very obvious and unacceptable; total before any deductions
exampleSentence: The company reported gross revenue of fifty million dollars last year.
exampleTranslation: 该公司去年报告总收入为五千万美元。

definition: To provide a formal assurance that something will happen
exampleSentence: The manufacturer guarantees the product against defects for two years.
exampleTranslation: 制造商保证产品在两年内没有缺陷。

definition: A person who protects or defends something; a legal custodian
exampleSentence: The court appointed a legal guardian for the orphaned children.
exampleTranslation: 法院为孤儿指定了法定监护人。

definition: The fact of having committed an offense; a feeling of blame
exampleSentence: The evidence proved his guilt beyond any reasonable doubt.
exampleTranslation: 证据确凿地证明了他的罪行。

definition: The natural home or environment of an animal or plant
exampleSentence: Deforestation is destroying the natural habitat of many species.
exampleTranslation: 森林砍伐正在破坏许多物种的自然栖息地。

definition: To bring or come to a sudden stop; to cease movement
exampleSentence: The government decided to halt all construction in the protected area.
exampleTranslation: 政府决定停止保护区内的所有施工。

definition: To hinder or impede the movement or progress of something
exampleSentence: Bad weather conditions hampered the rescue operation significantly.
exampleTranslation: 恶劣的天气条件严重妨碍了救援行动。

definition: A condition that limits someone's ability to function normally
exampleSentence: The new law prohibits discrimination based on physical handicap.
exampleTranslation: 新法律禁止基于身体残疾的歧视。

definition: Aggressive pressure or intimidation directed at someone
exampleSentence: The company has a zero-tolerance policy toward workplace harassment.
exampleTranslation: 公司对工作场所骚扰采取零容忍政策。

definition: The physical components of a computer or electronic system
exampleSentence: The company invested heavily in upgrading its computer hardware systems.
exampleTranslation: 公司大力投资升级其计算机硬件系统。

definition: The state of being in agreement or a pleasing arrangement
exampleSentence: Different cultures can live together in harmony with mutual respect.
exampleTranslation: 不同文化可以在相互尊重中和谐共处。

definition: Unpleasantly rough or cruel; severe and uncomfortable
exampleSentence: The harsh winter conditions made travel extremely dangerous.
exampleTranslation: 严酷的冬季条件使旅行极为危险。

definition: The process of gathering crops; the season's yield of crops
exampleSentence: The farmers celebrated an excellent harvest after months of hard labor.
exampleTranslation: 经过数月的辛劳，农民们庆祝了丰收。

definition: To continually trouble the mind or to appear as a ghost
exampleSentence: Memories of the war continued to haunt the survivors for decades.
exampleTranslation: 战争的记忆在几十年里持续困扰着幸存者。

definition: A danger or risk that could cause harm or injury
exampleSentence: Smoking is a serious health hazard that affects millions worldwide.
exampleTranslation: 吸烟是一种严重的健康危害，影响着全世界数百万人。

definition: A heading at the top of a newspaper article in large print
exampleSentence: The scandal made headlines in newspapers across the entire country.
exampleTranslation: 这一丑闻登上了全国各地报纸的头条。

definition: The main offices of an organization or the center of operations
exampleSentence: The company moved its headquarters to a new city last month.
exampleTranslation: 公司上个月将总部搬到了一个新城市。

definition: An untidy collection of things piled up in large quantity
exampleSentence: A heap of garbage was left on the street after the festival.
exampleTranslation: 节日过后，街上留下了一堆垃圾。

definition: To make or become more intense, stronger, or more acute
exampleSentence: The recent events have heightened public awareness of cybersecurity.
exampleTranslation: 最近的事件提高了公众对网络安全的认识。

definition: A half of the earth divided by the equator or a meridian
exampleSentence: Most of the world's population lives in the northern hemisphere.
exampleTranslation: 世界上大部分人口居住在北半球。

definition: Valued objects and traditions passed down through generations
exampleSentence: The ancient temple is an important part of the national cultural heritage.
exampleTranslation: 这座古庙是国家文化遗产的重要组成部分。

definition: Having the characteristics of a hero; showing great bravery
exampleSentence: The firefighters made a heroic effort to save the trapped residents.
exampleTranslation: 消防员英勇地努力营救被困的居民。

definition: A system in which members are ranked according to status
exampleSentence: The corporate hierarchy determines who reports to whom in the company.
exampleTranslation: 公司的等级制度决定了谁向谁汇报。

definition: To draw special attention to something important or notable
exampleSentence: The report highlights the need for greater investment in education.
exampleTranslation: 报告强调了加大教育投资的必要性。

definition: To create difficulties resulting in delay or obstruction
exampleSentence: Heavy regulations may hinder innovation in the technology sector.
exampleTranslation: 繁重的监管可能会妨碍科技领域的创新。

definition: Famous or important in history; having great significance
definitionZh: 历史性的；有历史意义的
exampleSentence: The peace agreement was a historic achievement for both nations.
exampleTranslation: 和平协议对两国来说都是一项历史性成就。

definition: Having a hole or empty space inside; without real significance
exampleSentence: The politician's promises turned out to be completely hollow.
exampleTranslation: 政治家的承诺结果完全是空洞的。

definition: Special honor or respect shown publicly to someone or something
exampleSentence: The ceremony paid homage to the veterans who served the country.
exampleTranslation: 仪式向曾为国家服务的退伍军人致敬。

definition: Of the same kind; alike in structure or composition
exampleSentence: The population of the small town is relatively homogeneous in origin.
exampleTranslation: 这个小镇的人口在来源上相对同质。

definition: Given as an honor without fulfilling the usual requirements
exampleSentence: The university awarded an honorary doctorate to the famous scientist.
exampleTranslation: 大学授予这位著名科学家荣誉博士学位。

definition: The line where earth and sky appear to meet; scope of interest
exampleSentence: New opportunities appeared on the horizon as technology advanced.
exampleTranslation: 随着技术进步，新的机遇出现在视野中。

definition: A regulatory substance produced in the body to stimulate cells
exampleSentence: Stress hormones can negatively affect the immune system over time.
exampleTranslation: 压力激素随着时间的推移会对免疫系统产生负面影响。

definition: An intense feeling of fear, shock, or disgust
exampleSentence: The refugees described the horror of fleeing their war-torn homeland.
exampleTranslation: 难民们描述了逃离饱受战争蹂躏的家园的恐惧。

definition: The friendly and generous reception of guests or strangers
exampleSentence: The country is famous for its warm hospitality toward foreign visitors.
exampleTranslation: 这个国家以对外国游客的热情好客而闻名。

definition: Unfriendly and opposed to something; aggressive in nature
exampleSentence: The hostile business environment forced many companies to relocate.
exampleTranslation: 敌对的商业环境迫使许多公司搬迁。

definition: The human race collectively; the quality of being humane
exampleSentence: Advances in medicine have greatly benefited all of humanity.
exampleTranslation: 医学进步极大地造福了全人类。

definition: Having a modest or low estimate of one's own importance
exampleSentence: Despite his great success he remained humble and approachable.
exampleTranslation: 尽管取得了巨大的成功，他仍然保持谦逊和平易近人。

definition: A thing made by combining two different elements or species
exampleSentence: The hybrid car uses both electricity and gasoline for propulsion.
exampleTranslation: 这辆混合动力汽车同时使用电力和汽油作为动力。

definition: Conditions or practices conducive to maintaining health
exampleSentence: Good personal hygiene is essential for preventing the spread of disease.
exampleTranslation: 良好的个人卫生对预防疾病传播至关重要。

definition: A proposed explanation made as a starting point for investigation
exampleSentence: The scientist tested the hypothesis through a series of experiments.
exampleTranslation: 科学家通过一系列实验验证了这一假设。

definition: Exaggerated or uncontrollable emotion or excitement
exampleSentence: The media coverage created mass hysteria about the new virus.
exampleTranslation: 媒体报道引发了公众对新病毒的大规模恐慌。

definition: Similar in every detail; exactly alike and indistinguishable
exampleSentence: The twins looked so identical that even their parents confused them.
exampleTranslation: 这对双胞胎长得太像了，连父母都分不清。

definition: A system of ideas and ideals forming the basis of a theory
exampleSentence: The political ideology of the party shifted dramatically over time.
exampleTranslation: 该党的政治意识形态随着时间发生了巨大变化。

definition: Not working or being used; without purpose or effect
exampleSentence: The machines sat idle during the long factory maintenance shutdown.
exampleTranslation: 在工厂长时间维修停工期间，机器闲置着。

definition: Lack of knowledge or information about something important
exampleSentence: Ignorance of the law is not considered a valid legal defense.
exampleTranslation: 对法律的无知不被视为有效的法律抗辩。

definition: To light up an area or to make something clearer to understand
exampleSentence: The research illuminated previously unknown aspects of the disease.
exampleTranslation: 这项研究阐明了该疾病以前未知的方面。

definition: A false idea or belief; a deceptive appearance or impression
exampleSentence: The magician created the illusion of making objects disappear instantly.
exampleTranslation: 魔术师制造了让物体瞬间消失的幻觉。

definition: To explain or make clear by using examples or visual aids
exampleSentence: The teacher used diagrams to illustrate the complex scientific concept.
exampleTranslation: 老师用图表来说明复杂的科学概念。

definition: Visually descriptive language or mental images collectively
exampleSentence: The poet uses vivid imagery to bring natural scenes to life.
exampleTranslation: 诗人运用生动的意象使自然景色栩栩如生。

definition: Extremely large or great in size, scale, or degree
exampleSentence: The project required an immense amount of planning and coordination.
exampleTranslation: 该项目需要大量的规划和协调。

definition: To dip or submerge in liquid or to involve deeply in activity
exampleSentence: Students are encouraged to immerse themselves in the local culture.
exampleTranslation: 鼓励学生沉浸在当地文化中。

definition: About to happen very soon; impending and unavoidable
exampleSentence: Authorities warned of imminent flooding in the low-lying coastal areas.
exampleTranslation: 当局警告低洼沿海地区即将发生洪水。

definition: Resistant to a particular infection or protected from something
exampleSentence: The vaccine helps the body become immune to the dangerous virus.
exampleTranslation: 疫苗帮助身体对这种危险病毒产生免疫力。

definition: The ability to resist infection or the state of being exempt
exampleSentence: The diplomat was granted immunity from prosecution while serving abroad.
exampleTranslation: 外交官在国外任职期间获得了起诉豁免权。

definition: The action of one object coming forcibly into contact; strong effect
exampleSentence: Technology has had a profound impact on modern education methods.
exampleTranslation: 技术对现代教育方法产生了深远的影响。

definition: To weaken or damage something, especially a faculty or function
exampleSentence: Excessive noise can impair hearing ability over a long period.
exampleTranslation: 过度噪音长期下来会损害听力。

definition: Relating to an empire or its ruler; majestic and commanding
exampleSentence: The imperial palace has been preserved as a historical monument.
exampleTranslation: 皇宫已被保留为历史古迹。

definition: To put a plan or decision into effect; to carry out
exampleSentence: The school will implement a new curriculum starting next semester.
exampleTranslation: 学校将从下学期开始实施新的课程。

definition: A likely consequence of something or the meaning behind words
exampleSentence: The policy change has serious implications for small business owners.
exampleTranslation: 政策变化对小企业主有严重影响。

definition: Implied though not directly expressed; absolute and unquestioning
exampleSentence: There was an implicit agreement that both parties would cooperate.
exampleTranslation: 双方有一个心照不宣的合作协议。

definition: To force something unwelcome to be accepted or put in place
exampleSentence: The government decided to impose higher taxes on luxury imported goods.
exampleTranslation: 政府决定对进口奢侈品征收更高的税。

definition: A sudden strong urge to act or a driving force in physics
exampleSentence: She bought the expensive dress on impulse without thinking twice.
exampleTranslation: 她一时冲动买了那条昂贵的裙子。

definition: Not sufficient or not good enough for a particular purpose
exampleSentence: The school's facilities are inadequate for the growing number of students.
exampleTranslation: 学校的设施不足以满足日益增长的学生数量。

definition: To begin or introduce a new system or to admit to office
exampleSentence: The president was inaugurated in a grand ceremony at the capital.
exampleTranslation: 总统在首都的盛大仪式上就职。

definition: A thing that motivates or encourages someone to do something
exampleSentence: Tax incentives encourage businesses to invest in renewable energy.
exampleTranslation: 税收优惠鼓励企业投资可再生能源。

definition: The occurrence or rate of something happening in a population
exampleSentence: The incidence of diabetes has increased dramatically in recent years.
exampleTranslation: 近年来糖尿病的发病率急剧增加。

definition: A tendency or natural disposition toward a particular behavior
exampleSentence: She showed a strong inclination toward studying the natural sciences.
exampleTranslation: 她表现出对自然科学的强烈兴趣。

definition: Including all services or groups; not excluding any particular part
exampleSentence: The organization promotes an inclusive environment for all members.
exampleTranslation: 该组织为所有成员营造包容的环境。

definition: To take in or include as part of a whole or group
exampleSentence: The updated plan incorporates feedback received from all stakeholders.
exampleTranslation: 更新后的计划纳入了所有利益相关者的反馈。

definition: Impossible or very hard to believe; extraordinarily good
exampleSentence: The team made an incredible comeback to win the championship game.
exampleTranslation: 团队实现了令人难以置信的逆转赢得了冠军赛。

definition: An alphabetical list of topics with references in a publication
exampleSentence: The consumer price index rose by three percent over the past year.
exampleTranslation: 消费者价格指数在过去一年中上升了百分之三。

definition: To point out or show something; to be a sign of
exampleSentence: Recent studies indicate a strong link between diet and health.
exampleTranslation: 最近的研究表明饮食与健康之间有很强的联系。

definition: Having no particular interest or sympathy; unconcerned
exampleSentence: The public remained largely indifferent to the political debate.
exampleTranslation: 公众对这场政治辩论基本上漠不关心。

definition: Originating or occurring naturally in a particular place
exampleSentence: The project aims to protect the rights of indigenous communities.
exampleTranslation: 该项目旨在保护土著社区的权利。

definition: Feeling or showing anger about something unjust or unworthy
exampleSentence: Citizens were indignant about the government's response to the crisis.
exampleTranslation: 市民对政府应对危机的方式感到愤慨。

definition: Absolutely necessary and too important to do without
definitionZh: 不可缺少的；必不可少的
exampleSentence: Clean water is indispensable for maintaining good public health.
exampleTranslation: 清洁的水对维持良好的公共健康是必不可少的。

definition: To succeed in persuading someone or to bring about a result
exampleSentence: Stress can induce a variety of physical and mental health problems.
exampleTranslation: 压力会导致各种身体和心理健康问题。

definition: Certain to happen and unable to be avoided or prevented
exampleSentence: Change is inevitable in a rapidly evolving technological landscape.
exampleTranslation: 在快速发展的技术环境中，变化是不可避免的。

definition: A very young child in the earliest stage of life
exampleSentence: The clinic provides free healthcare services for infants and mothers.
exampleTranslation: 诊所为婴儿和母亲提供免费医疗服务。

definition: Lower in rank, quality, or status compared to another
exampleSentence: The counterfeit product is clearly inferior in quality to the original.
exampleTranslation: 假冒产品的质量明显不如正品。

definition: Limitless or endless in space, extent, or size
exampleSentence: The universe appears to contain an infinite number of galaxies.
exampleTranslation: 宇宙似乎包含无限数量的星系。

definition: A general increase in prices and fall in purchasing value
exampleSentence: Rising inflation has eroded the purchasing power of average consumers.
exampleTranslation: 不断上升的通货膨胀削弱了普通消费者的购买力。

definition: To cause something unpleasant to be suffered by someone
exampleSentence: The hurricane inflicted severe damage on the coastal communities.
exampleTranslation: 飓风对沿海社区造成了严重破坏。

definition: Having great influence on someone or something important
exampleSentence: She is one of the most influential figures in modern technology.
exampleTranslation: 她是现代科技领域最有影响力的人物之一。

definition: The basic physical systems of a country or organization
exampleSentence: The government plans to invest billions in improving national infrastructure.
exampleTranslation: 政府计划投资数十亿改善国家基础设施。

definition: Cleverly inventive or resourceful in design or execution
exampleSentence: The engineer proposed an ingenious solution to the complex problem.
exampleTranslation: 工程师为这个复杂问题提出了一个巧妙的解决方案。

definition: To live in or occupy a particular place as a resident
exampleSentence: Many rare species inhabit the remote tropical rainforest.
exampleTranslation: 许多珍稀物种栖息在偏远的热带雨林中。

definition: Existing as a natural and permanent quality of something
exampleSentence: There are inherent risks associated with any major surgical procedure.
exampleTranslation: 任何重大外科手术都存在固有的风险。

definition: To hinder or restrain an action, process, or development
exampleSentence: The drug is designed to inhibit the growth of cancer cells.
exampleTranslation: 该药物旨在抑制癌细胞的生长。

definition: Existing or occurring at the beginning of a process
exampleSentence: The initial results of the experiment were very encouraging.
exampleTranslation: 实验的初步结果非常令人鼓舞。

definition: To cause a process or action to begin formally
exampleSentence: The organization decided to initiate a new community outreach program.
exampleTranslation: 该组织决定发起一项新的社区推广计划。

definition: To introduce a substance into the body or to add something
exampleSentence: The nurse will inject the vaccine into the patient's upper arm.
exampleTranslation: 护士将把疫苗注射到患者的上臂。

definition: Inborn; natural rather than acquired through learning
exampleSentence: Humans have an innate ability to learn and use complex language.
exampleTranslation: 人类具有学习和使用复杂语言的天生能力。

definition: Not guilty of a crime or free from moral wrong
exampleSentence: The jury found the defendant innocent of all criminal charges.
exampleTranslation: 陪审团裁定被告所有刑事指控无罪。

definition: A new method, idea, or product introduced to improve something
exampleSentence: Technological innovation drives economic growth and social progress.
exampleTranslation: 技术创新推动经济增长和社会进步。

definition: What is put in or contributed to a process or system
exampleSentence: We value the input of all team members in the decision process.
exampleTranslation: 我们重视所有团队成员在决策过程中的意见。

definition: An act of asking for information or an official investigation
exampleSentence: The police launched a formal inquiry into the cause of the fire.
exampleTranslation: 警方对火灾原因展开了正式调查。

definition: To place or fit something inside or into another thing
exampleSentence: Please insert your bank card into the machine to begin.
exampleTranslation: 请将银行卡插入机器开始操作。

definition: An accurate and deep understanding of a complex situation
exampleSentence: The book offers valuable insight into the workings of modern politics.
exampleTranslation: 这本书对现代政治的运作提供了宝贵的见解。

definition: To look at something closely to assess quality or condition
exampleSentence: Health officials will inspect the restaurant for safety compliance.
exampleTranslation: 卫生官员将检查该餐厅是否符合安全规定。

definition: To fill someone with the urge to do or feel something creative
exampleSentence: The teacher's passion for science inspired many students to study it.
exampleTranslation: 老师对科学的热情激励了许多学生学习科学。

definition: To place equipment in position for use; to set up software
exampleSentence: The technician will install the new security system tomorrow morning.
exampleTranslation: 技术人员明天早上将安装新的安全系统。

definition: A sum of money due as part of a series of payments
exampleSentence: You can pay for the car in twelve monthly installments.
exampleTranslation: 您可以分十二期按月付款购买这辆车。

definition: An example or single occurrence of something happening
exampleSentence: In this instance the court ruled in favor of the defendant.
exampleTranslation: 在这个案例中法院做出了有利于被告的裁决。

definition: Happening or produced immediately without any delay
exampleSentence: The news spread across social media in an instant.
exampleTranslation: 这条新闻瞬间在社交媒体上传播开来。

definition: An organization established for a particular purpose
exampleSentence: The research institute published its findings in a major journal.
exampleTranslation: 研究所在一份重要期刊上发表了研究结果。

definition: To direct or command someone to do something; to teach
exampleSentence: The manual instructs users on how to operate the machine safely.
exampleTranslation: 手册指导用户如何安全操作机器。

definition: Not damaged or impaired; remaining complete and whole
exampleSentence: The ancient manuscript was found remarkably intact after centuries.
exampleTranslation: 这份古代手稿在几个世纪后被发现保存完好。

definition: Necessary to make a whole complete; essential and fundamental
exampleSentence: Teamwork is an integral part of any successful business operation.
exampleTranslation: 团队合作是任何成功商业运营中不可缺少的一部分。

definition: To combine things so they work together as a unified whole
exampleSentence: The company plans to integrate the new technology into existing systems.
exampleTranslation: 公司计划将新技术整合到现有系统中。

definition: Relating to the intellect or having a highly developed mind
exampleSentence: The university fosters intellectual growth through rigorous academic programs.
exampleTranslation: 大学通过严格的学术项目促进智力成长。

definition: Of extreme force, degree, or strength; very concentrated
exampleSentence: The intense heat wave caused widespread power outages across the region.
exampleTranslation: 强烈的热浪导致该地区大范围停电。

definition: To become or make more intense, stronger, or more extreme
exampleSentence: The storm is expected to intensify as it moves toward the coast.
exampleTranslation: 预计风暴在向海岸移动时将加剧。

definition: The aim or plan behind an action; determination to do something
exampleSentence: The court must determine the intent behind the defendant's actions.
exampleTranslation: 法院必须确定被告行为背后的意图。

definition: To communicate or work together with someone or something
exampleSentence: Children learn social skills by interacting with their peers at school.
exampleTranslation: 孩子们通过在学校与同龄人互动来学习社交技能。

definition: To stop or catch something before it reaches its destination
exampleSentence: The police managed to intercept the shipment of illegal goods.
exampleTranslation: 警方成功拦截了这批非法货物。

definition: To prevent something from continuing or being done properly
exampleSentence: The government should not interfere with the freedom of the press.
exampleTranslation: 政府不应干涉新闻自由。

definition: Provisional or temporary, serving during an intervening period
exampleSentence: An interim report will be released before the final version.
exampleTranslation: 在最终版本之前将发布一份临时报告。

definition: The inner part or inside of something; the inland region
exampleSentence: The interior of the building was completely renovated last year.
exampleTranslation: 建筑物的内部去年进行了全面翻新。

definition: Coming between two things in time, place, or level
exampleSentence: The course is designed for students at an intermediate English level.
exampleTranslation: 该课程专为中级英语水平的学生设计。

definition: To explain the meaning of something or to translate orally
exampleSentence: Different scholars interpret the historical evidence in different ways.
exampleTranslation: 不同的学者以不同的方式解读历史证据。

definition: To come between disputing parties to alter the outcome
exampleSentence: The United Nations decided to intervene in the regional conflict.
exampleTranslation: 联合国决定干预该地区冲突。

definition: The action of becoming involved in a situation to change it
exampleSentence: Early medical intervention can greatly improve patient outcomes.
exampleTranslation: 早期的医疗干预可以大大改善患者的预后。

definition: Very close and familiar; private and personal in nature
exampleSentence: Only a few intimate friends were invited to the small gathering.
exampleTranslation: 只有几位亲密的朋友被邀请参加这个小型聚会。

definition: Very complicated or detailed with many interrelated parts
exampleSentence: The watch features an intricate mechanism with hundreds of tiny parts.
exampleTranslation: 这块手表有一个由数百个小零件组成的复杂机芯。

definition: Belonging naturally or essentially to something from within
exampleSentence: Curiosity is an intrinsic part of human nature and development.
exampleTranslation: 好奇心是人类本性和发展的固有部分。

definition: To enter uninvited or to force oneself into a situation
exampleSentence: The new buildings intrude upon the natural beauty of the landscape.
exampleTranslation: 新建筑侵扰了风景的自然美。

definition: The ability to understand something immediately without reasoning
exampleSentence: She relied on her intuition to make the important business decision.
exampleTranslation: 她凭直觉做出了重要的商业决策。

definition: To enter a place or situation with force as an enemy
exampleSentence: The army crossed the border and attempted to invade the territory.
exampleTranslation: 军队越过边境试图入侵该领土。

definition: A complete list of items or goods held in stock
exampleSentence: The store conducts a complete inventory check at the end of year.
exampleTranslation: 商店在年底进行完整的库存检查。

definition: To carry out a systematic inquiry to establish facts
exampleSentence: The authorities will investigate the cause of the factory explosion.
exampleTranslation: 当局将调查工厂爆炸的原因。

definition: Unable to be seen; not easily noticed or detected
exampleSentence: The effects of air pollution are often invisible to the naked eye.
exampleTranslation: 空气污染的影响往往是肉眼看不见的。

definition: To cite or appeal to someone or something as an authority
exampleSentence: The lawyer invoked the constitutional right to free speech.
exampleTranslation: 律师援引了宪法规定的言论自由权利。

definition: To include something as a necessary part or to engage actively
exampleSentence: The project will involve collaboration between multiple departments.
exampleTranslation: 该项目将涉及多个部门之间的合作。

definition: The expression of meaning using language that normally means opposite
exampleSentence: The irony of the situation was that the fire station burned down.
exampleTranslation: 这种情况的讽刺之处在于消防站被烧毁了。

definition: Not even or balanced; contrary to rule or accepted standards
exampleSentence: The patient's heartbeat was irregular and required immediate attention.
exampleTranslation: 患者的心跳不规则，需要立即关注。

definition: Not connected with or applicable to the matter at hand
exampleSentence: The judge ruled that the evidence was irrelevant to the case.
exampleTranslation: 法官裁定该证据与案件无关。

definition: To make someone annoyed or to cause inflammation
exampleSentence: The chemical fumes can irritate the eyes and respiratory system.
exampleTranslation: 化学烟雾会刺激眼睛和呼吸系统。

definition: To cause something or someone to be alone or set apart
exampleSentence: Scientists managed to isolate the virus responsible for the outbreak.
exampleTranslation: 科学家成功分离了导致疫情的病毒。

definition: To put something or someone into a situation of danger or risk
exampleSentence: Careless mistakes could jeopardize the entire research project.
exampleTranslation: 粗心的错误可能会危及整个研究项目。

definition: The activity of writing for newspapers, magazines, or media
exampleSentence: Investigative journalism plays a vital role in holding power accountable.
exampleTranslation: 调查新闻在追究权力责任方面起着至关重要的作用。

definition: Relating to courts of law or the administration of justice
exampleSentence: The judicial system must be independent from political influence.
exampleTranslation: 司法系统必须独立于政治影响之外。

definition: The official power to make legal decisions in a defined area
exampleSentence: The case falls outside the jurisdiction of the local court.
exampleTranslation: 此案不在当地法院的管辖范围内。

definition: To show or prove to be right or reasonable in action
exampleSentence: The manager tried to justify the budget cuts to the employees.
exampleTranslation: 经理试图向员工证明预算削减的合理性。

definition: Having a sharp edge or enthusiastic and eager about something
exampleSentence: She has a keen interest in artificial intelligence and robotics.
exampleTranslation: 她对人工智能和机器人技术有浓厚的兴趣。

definition: To take someone away illegally by force, often for ransom
exampleSentence: The businessman was kidnapped and held for a large ransom.
exampleTranslation: 这位商人被绑架并被索要巨额赎金。

definition: A small piece of paper or fabric attached giving information
exampleSentence: Always read the nutrition label before purchasing packaged food items.
exampleTranslation: 购买包装食品前请务必阅读营养标签。

definition: To fall behind in movement or development compared to others
exampleSentence: Rural areas often lag behind urban centers in economic development.
exampleTranslation: 农村地区在经济发展方面往往落后于城市。

definition: All the visible features of an area of land; a broad view
exampleSentence: The political landscape has changed dramatically since the election.
exampleTranslation: 自选举以来，政治格局发生了巨大变化。

definition: Relating to or situated at the side of something
exampleSentence: The company encourages lateral thinking to solve complex problems.
exampleTranslation: 公司鼓励横向思维来解决复杂问题。

definition: To start or set in motion a new product, project, or venture
exampleSentence: The company plans to launch its new product line next quarter.
exampleTranslation: 公司计划在下季度推出新产品线。

definition: A claim or dispute brought to a court of law for judgment
exampleSentence: The customer filed a lawsuit against the company for negligence.
exampleTranslation: 客户因疏忽向该公司提起诉讼。

definition: A sheet or thickness of material covering a surface
exampleSentence: The cake has multiple layers of chocolate and cream filling.
exampleTranslation: 蛋糕有多层巧克力和奶油夹心。

definition: The way in which parts of something are arranged or set out
exampleSentence: The architect presented the layout of the new office building.
exampleTranslation: 建筑师展示了新办公楼的布局。

definition: A printed sheet of paper giving information or advertising
exampleSentence: Volunteers distributed leaflets about the upcoming community event.
exampleTranslation: 志愿者分发了关于即将举行的社区活动的传单。

definition: A contract granting use of property for a specified time period
exampleSentence: The company signed a five-year lease for the new office space.
exampleTranslation: 公司签订了新办公空间的五年租约。

definition: Laws collectively or the process of making new laws
exampleSentence: New legislation was introduced to protect consumer privacy rights.
exampleTranslation: 新立法被引入以保护消费者隐私权。

definition: Conforming to the law or to rules; reasonable and justified
exampleSentence: The company operates as a legitimate business with proper licenses.
exampleTranslation: 该公司以合法企业的身份经营，拥有适当的执照。

definition: Free time when one is not working or doing duties
exampleSentence: Most people spend their leisure time watching television or reading.
exampleTranslation: 大多数人在闲暇时间看电视或阅读。

definition: To impose a tax, fee, or fine on someone or something
exampleSentence: The government decided to levy a new tax on sugary beverages.
exampleTranslation: 政府决定对含糖饮料征收新税。

definition: Responsible by law or likely to do or experience something
exampleSentence: The manufacturer is liable for any defects found in the product.
exampleTranslation: 制造商对产品中发现的任何缺陷负有责任。

definition: Open to new ideas; favoring individual freedom and reform
exampleSentence: The university is known for its liberal approach to education.
exampleTranslation: 这所大学以其开明的教育方式而闻名。

definition: In the same way; also and in addition to what was said
exampleSentence: The new policy benefits employees; likewise it improves company efficiency.
exampleTranslation: 新政策惠及员工；同样也提高了公司效率。

definition: The ability to read and write or competence in a subject
exampleSentence: Digital literacy is now considered essential in the modern workforce.
exampleTranslation: 数字素养现在被认为在现代劳动力中是必不可少的。

definition: In a literal manner or sense; exactly as stated
exampleSentence: The town was literally destroyed by the powerful tornado last night.
exampleTranslation: 昨晚这个小镇确实被强大的龙卷风摧毁了。

definition: To discover the exact position of something or to establish
exampleSentence: The rescue team was unable to locate the missing hikers.
exampleTranslation: 救援队无法找到失踪的徒步旅行者。

definition: A powered vehicle used for pulling trains on railway tracks
exampleSentence: The old steam locomotive has been restored and put on display.
exampleTranslation: 这台旧蒸汽机车已被修复并展出。

definition: Of imposing height or elevated in character and spirit
exampleSentence: The organization has lofty goals of eliminating poverty worldwide.
exampleTranslation: 该组织有消除全球贫困的崇高目标。

definition: Based on clear and sound reasoning; following natural order
exampleSentence: The conclusion follows as the logical result of the given evidence.
exampleTranslation: 结论是所给证据的逻辑结果。

definition: To appear as a shadowy or threatening form; to seem imminent
exampleSentence: The threat of an economic recession looms over the global markets.
exampleTranslation: 经济衰退的威胁笼罩着全球市场。

definition: A strong feeling of support or allegiance to someone
exampleSentence: Customer loyalty is built through consistent quality and good service.
exampleTranslation: 客户忠诚度是通过一贯的质量和良好的服务建立的。

definition: A state of great comfort and elegance involving great expense
exampleSentence: The luxury hotel offers guests personalized service and gourmet dining.
exampleTranslation: 这家豪华酒店为客人提供个性化服务和美食。

definition: The great size or extent of something; relative importance
exampleSentence: Few people understood the magnitude of the environmental crisis.
exampleTranslation: 很少有人理解环境危机的严重程度。

definition: The ideas or activities shared by most people in a society
exampleSentence: Electric vehicles have now entered the mainstream consumer market.
exampleTranslation: 电动汽车现在已进入主流消费市场。

definition: To cause something to continue in its existing state
exampleSentence: The government must maintain public order during the transition period.
exampleTranslation: 政府必须在过渡期间维持公共秩序。

definition: To handle or control something skillfully or to influence unfairly
exampleSentence: The politician was accused of trying to manipulate public opinion.
exampleTranslation: 这位政治家被指控试图操纵公众舆论。

definition: Done with the hands or relating to physical work
exampleSentence: The instruction manual explains how to assemble the furniture correctly.
exampleTranslation: 说明手册解释了如何正确组装家具。

definition: An author's original text before printing or a handwritten document
exampleSentence: The ancient manuscript was discovered in a monastery library.
exampleTranslation: 这份古代手稿是在一座修道院图书馆中发现的。

definition: The edge or border of something; the amount of difference
exampleSentence: The company's profit margin increased significantly this quarter.
exampleTranslation: 公司本季度的利润率显著提高。

definition: Relating to or found in the sea; connected with seafaring
exampleSentence: Marine pollution threatens thousands of species living in the ocean.
exampleTranslation: 海洋污染威胁着生活在海洋中的数千种物种。

definition: A system of parts working together in a machine or process
exampleSentence: Scientists are studying the mechanism by which the virus spreads.
exampleTranslation: 科学家正在研究病毒传播的机制。

definition: Relating to the Middle Ages in European history
exampleSentence: The medieval castle has been well preserved for over eight centuries.
exampleTranslation: 这座中世纪城堡已保存了八百多年。

definition: A means of communication or a middle state between extremes
exampleSentence: Television remains an influential medium for news and entertainment.
exampleTranslation: 电视仍然是新闻和娱乐的有影响力的媒介。

definition: A thin layer of tissue or material acting as a boundary
exampleSentence: The cell membrane controls what enters and exits the cell.
exampleTranslation: 细胞膜控制着进出细胞的物质。

definition: The quality of being worthy or having value; a good point
exampleSentence: The proposal will be judged entirely on its own individual merit.
exampleTranslation: 该提案将完全根据其自身的优点来评判。

definition: Relating to a large city and its surrounding suburbs
exampleSentence: The metropolitan area has a population of over ten million people.
exampleTranslation: 大都市区的人口超过一千万。

definition: To move from one region or country to settle in another
exampleSentence: Millions of birds migrate south every year to escape the winter.
exampleTranslation: 每年数百万只鸟向南迁徙以躲避冬天。

definition: Relating to or characteristic of soldiers or armed forces
exampleSentence: The country increased its military spending by fifteen percent.
exampleTranslation: 该国将军事开支增加了百分之十五。

definition: To reduce something to the smallest possible amount or degree
exampleSentence: The company took steps to minimize the environmental impact of operations.
exampleTranslation: 公司采取措施最大限度地减少运营对环境的影响。

definition: An extraordinary event that cannot be explained naturally
exampleSentence: The doctors called the patient's full recovery nothing short of miracle.
exampleTranslation: 医生称患者的完全康复简直是个奇迹。

definition: Average in amount or intensity; not extreme in views
exampleSentence: The doctor recommended moderate exercise three times per week.
exampleTranslation: 医生建议每周进行三次适度运动。

definition: To make partial changes to something to improve or adapt it
exampleSentence: The engineers had to modify the original design to meet standards.
exampleTranslation: 工程师不得不修改原始设计以满足标准。

definition: A group of atoms bonded together representing the smallest unit
exampleSentence: Water is a molecule composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen.
exampleTranslation: 水是由两个氢原子和一个氧原子组成的分子。

definition: To observe and check the progress of something over time
exampleSentence: Doctors will monitor the patient's condition closely after surgery.
exampleTranslation: 医生将在手术后密切监控患者的状况。

definition: The exclusive control of a commodity or service in a market
exampleSentence: The company was accused of having a monopoly in the software market.
exampleTranslation: 该公司被指控在软件市场上垄断。

definition: Concerned with principles of right and wrong behavior
exampleSentence: The story teaches an important moral lesson about honesty and trust.
exampleTranslation: 这个故事教授了关于诚实和信任的重要道德课程。

definition: To provide someone with a reason for doing something
exampleSentence: Good leaders know how to motivate their teams to achieve goals.
exampleTranslation: 好的领导者知道如何激励团队实现目标。

definition: Having or involving several parts, elements, or members
exampleSentence: The suspect was charged with multiple counts of financial fraud.
exampleTranslation: 嫌疑人被指控犯有多项金融欺诈罪。

definition: Relating to a city or town and its local government
exampleSentence: The municipal government approved funding for a new public library.
exampleTranslation: 市政府批准了新公共图书馆的拨款。

definition: Experienced or done by each of two parties toward the other
exampleSentence: The agreement was based on mutual respect and shared interests.
exampleTranslation: 协议是基于相互尊重和共同利益的。

definition: That is to say; used to give specific details about something
exampleSentence: Two factors contributed to success, namely hard work and innovation.
exampleTranslation: 两个因素促成了成功，即努力工作和创新。

definition: To find one's way or to plan and direct the course of travel
exampleSentence: It can be difficult to navigate the complex bureaucratic system.
exampleTranslation: 在复杂的官僚体系中摸索可能很困难。

definition: So small or unimportant as to be not worth considering
exampleSentence: The difference in performance between the two models is negligible.
exampleTranslation: 两种型号之间的性能差异微不足道。

definition: To discuss something to reach an agreement between parties
exampleSentence: The union plans to negotiate with management over higher wages.
exampleTranslation: 工会计划与管理层就提高工资进行谈判。

definition: Not supporting either side in a conflict; impartial
exampleSentence: The country maintained a neutral position during the international dispute.
exampleTranslation: 该国在国际争端中保持了中立立场。

definition: In spite of that; nevertheless or even so
exampleSentence: The task was difficult; nonetheless the team completed it on time.
exampleTranslation: 任务很困难，尽管如此团队还是按时完成了。

definition: A standard or pattern that is typical or expected behavior
exampleSentence: Working from home has become the norm in many industries.
exampleTranslation: 在家工作已成为许多行业的常态。

definition: Worthy of attention or remarkable; distinguished
exampleSentence: The scientist made several notable contributions to medical research.
exampleTranslation: 这位科学家对医学研究做出了几项显著贡献。

definition: To formally inform someone of something important
exampleSentence: The school will notify parents of any schedule changes by email.
exampleTranslation: 学校将通过电子邮件通知家长任何日程变更。

definition: A conception of or belief about something; a vague idea
exampleSentence: The notion that money buys happiness has been widely challenged.
exampleTranslation: 金钱买来幸福的观念已被广泛质疑。

definition: In spite of; without being affected by something
exampleSentence: Notwithstanding the challenges the project was completed successfully.
exampleTranslation: 尽管面临挑战，项目还是成功完成了。

definition: To provide food or substances necessary for growth and health
exampleSentence: A balanced diet is essential to nourish the body and mind.
exampleTranslation: 均衡的饮食对滋养身心至关重要。

definition: New or unusual in an interesting way; not previously known
exampleSentence: The researcher proposed a novel approach to solving the energy crisis.
exampleTranslation: 研究人员提出了解决能源危机的新颖方法。

definition: Great in number; consisting of many members or items
exampleSentence: Numerous studies have confirmed the health benefits of regular exercise.
exampleTranslation: 众多研究证实了定期锻炼的健康益处。

definition: To care for and encourage the growth or development of
exampleSentence: Parents play a crucial role in nurturing their children's talents.
exampleTranslation: 父母在培养孩子才能方面起着至关重要的作用。

definition: A goal or aim to be achieved; not influenced by personal feelings
exampleSentence: The main objective of the project is to reduce carbon emissions.
exampleTranslation: 该项目的主要目标是减少碳排放。

definition: To require or compel someone to do something by law or duty
exampleSentence: The law obliges employers to provide a safe working environment.
exampleTranslation: 法律要求雇主提供安全的工作环境。

definition: Not clearly expressed or easily understood; not well known
exampleSentence: The meaning of the ancient text remains obscure to modern scholars.
exampleTranslation: 这篇古文的含义对现代学者来说仍然模糊不清。

definition: No longer produced or used; out of date and old-fashioned
exampleSentence: Rapid technological advances have made many older devices obsolete.
exampleTranslation: 快速的技术进步已使许多旧设备过时。

definition: To block or get in the way of something moving or progressing
exampleSentence: Fallen trees obstructed the main road after the violent storm.
exampleTranslation: 暴风雨后倒下的树木堵塞了主要道路。

definition: To get or acquire something through effort or request
exampleSentence: Students must obtain permission before accessing the restricted area.
exampleTranslation: 学生在进入限制区域之前必须获得许可。

definition: A particular time when something happens; a special event
exampleSentence: The graduation ceremony was a memorable occasion for all families.
exampleTranslation: 毕业典礼对所有家庭来说都是一个难忘的场合。

definition: To reside or fill a space; to hold a position or job
exampleSentence: The new factory will occupy a large area on the outskirts.
exampleTranslation: 新工厂将占据郊区的一大片区域。

definition: To happen or take place; to come to mind suddenly
exampleSentence: Natural disasters can occur without any advance warning at all.
exampleTranslation: 自然灾害可能在毫无预警的情况下发生。

definition: To counterbalance or compensate for something negative
exampleSentence: The cost savings will offset the initial investment within two years.
exampleTranslation: 成本节约将在两年内抵消初始投资。

definition: To leave out or exclude something, either deliberately or not
exampleSentence: The report omitted several important facts about the investigation.
exampleTranslation: 报告省略了有关调查的几个重要事实。

definition: Continuing to exist or progress without interruption
exampleSentence: The ongoing negotiations between the two countries show positive signs.
exampleTranslation: 两国之间正在进行的谈判显示出积极的迹象。

definition: A person who competes against another in a contest or debate
exampleSentence: The chess player defeated her opponent in just twenty-five moves.
exampleTranslation: 这位棋手仅用二十五步就击败了对手。

definition: Hopeful and confident about the future or a positive outcome
exampleSentence: The CEO remains optimistic about the company's growth prospects.
exampleTranslation: 首席执行官对公司的增长前景仍然保持乐观。

definition: A thing that is or may be chosen; the freedom to choose
exampleSentence: Customers have the option of paying by credit card or cash.
exampleTranslation: 客户可以选择用信用卡或现金支付。

definition: The curved path of a celestial body around a star or planet
exampleSentence: The satellite was successfully placed into orbit around the Earth.
exampleTranslation: 卫星被成功送入绕地球的轨道。

definition: The determination of one's position or a training introduction
exampleSentence: All new employees must attend a two-day orientation program.
exampleTranslation: 所有新员工必须参加为期两天的入职培训。

definition: To have one's origin in a particular place or situation
exampleSentence: The tradition is believed to originate from ancient religious practices.
exampleTranslation: 这一传统被认为起源于古代宗教习俗。

definition: A sudden occurrence of something unwelcome such as disease
exampleSentence: Health authorities responded quickly to the outbreak of the disease.
exampleTranslation: 卫生当局对疾病的爆发迅速做出了反应。

definition: The way something turns out; a result or consequence
exampleSentence: The outcome of the election surprised most political commentators.
exampleTranslation: 选举结果让大多数政治评论员感到意外。

definition: An extremely strong reaction of anger or indignation
exampleSentence: The decision provoked outrage among environmental protection groups.
exampleTranslation: 这一决定在环保团体中引发了愤怒。

definition: To be heavier or more significant than something else
exampleSentence: The benefits of the new system clearly outweigh its potential risks.
exampleTranslation: 新系统的好处显然超过了其潜在风险。

definition: Taken as a whole; including everything considered together
exampleSentence: The overall quality of education has improved significantly this decade.
exampleTranslation: 本十年教育的整体质量显著提高。

definition: To succeed in dealing with a problem or difficulty
exampleSentence: With determination she managed to overcome all the obstacles.
exampleTranslation: 凭借决心，她成功克服了所有障碍。

definition: To fail to notice or to have a view from above
exampleSentence: It is easy to overlook small details when you are in a hurry.
exampleTranslation: 当你匆忙时很容易忽略小细节。

definition: In or to a foreign country across the sea; abroad
exampleSentence: Many students choose to study overseas for broader career opportunities.
exampleTranslation: 许多学生选择出国留学以获得更广阔的职业机会。

definition: Very great in amount or very strong in effect
exampleSentence: The evidence against the suspect was absolutely overwhelming.
exampleTranslation: 针对嫌疑人的证据是绝对压倒性的。

definition: A chemical compound of oxygen with another element
exampleSentence: Carbon dioxide is the most common oxide in Earth's atmosphere.
exampleTranslation: 二氧化碳是地球大气中最常见的氧化物。

definition: A seemingly contradictory statement that may reveal a truth
exampleSentence: It is a paradox that technology connects yet isolates people.
exampleTranslation: 科技使人联系更紧密却又更孤立，这是一个悖论。

definition: Occurring at the same time and having similarities
exampleSentence: The two countries followed parallel paths of economic development.
exampleTranslation: 两国走了相似的经济发展道路。

definition: To take part or become involved in an activity or event
exampleSentence: All employees are encouraged to participate in the training program.
exampleTranslation: 鼓励所有员工参加培训计划。

definition: A government license giving exclusive rights for an invention
exampleSentence: The inventor filed a patent for the new energy-saving technology.
exampleTranslation: 发明者为这项新的节能技术申请了专利。

definition: Having devotion and vigorous support for one's country
exampleSentence: The patriotic ceremony honored the soldiers who served the nation.
exampleTranslation: 爱国仪式向曾为国家服务的军人致敬。

definition: Strange or odd; belonging exclusively to a particular person
exampleSentence: The animal has a peculiar diet consisting almost entirely of bamboo.
exampleTranslation: 这种动物有一种奇特的饮食，几乎完全由竹子组成。

definition: A punishment imposed for breaking a law or rule
exampleSentence: The penalty for tax evasion can include heavy fines and prison.
exampleTranslation: 逃税的惩罚可包括重罚款和监禁。

definition: To succeed in entering or passing through something
exampleSentence: The company is trying to penetrate the lucrative Asian market.
exampleTranslation: 该公司正试图打入利润丰厚的亚洲市场。

definition: To become aware of something through the senses or mind
exampleSentence: People perceive the world differently based on their cultural background.
exampleTranslation: 人们根据自己的文化背景以不同方式感知世界。

definition: Lasting or intended to last indefinitely without change
exampleSentence: The company offered her a permanent position after the internship.
exampleTranslation: 公司在实习后给她提供了一个永久职位。

definition: To spread throughout something; to pass through a substance
exampleSentence: The smell of fresh coffee permeated the entire office building.
exampleTranslation: 新鲜咖啡的气味弥漫了整栋办公楼。

definition: Never ending or changing; occurring repeatedly without stop
exampleSentence: The region has been in a state of perpetual conflict for decades.
exampleTranslation: 该地区几十年来一直处于持续冲突的状态。

definition: Continuing firmly in a course of action despite difficulty
exampleSentence: Her persistent efforts finally paid off with a major breakthrough.
exampleTranslation: 她坚持不懈的努力终于取得了重大突破。

definition: A particular attitude toward something; a point of view
exampleSentence: The book offers a fresh perspective on the history of civilization.
exampleTranslation: 这本书提供了对文明史的全新视角。

definition: A formal written request appealing to authority for a cause
exampleSentence: Over ten thousand citizens signed the petition against the new law.
exampleTranslation: 超过一万名市民签署了反对新法律的请愿书。

definition: A fact or event observed to exist or happen; a remarkable thing
exampleSentence: Global warming is a well-documented scientific phenomenon studied widely.
exampleTranslation: 全球变暖是一个有据可查的、被广泛研究的科学现象。

definition: A person who is among the first to develop something new
exampleSentence: She was a pioneer in the field of computer science and research.
exampleTranslation: 她是计算机科学和研究领域的先驱。

definition: Seeming reasonable or probable; appearing worthy of belief
exampleSentence: The detective found no plausible explanation for the missing evidence.
exampleTranslation: 侦探找不到失踪证据的合理解释。

definition: To make a solemn promise or commitment to do something
exampleSentence: The government pledged to invest more money in public healthcare.
exampleTranslation: 政府承诺在公共医疗保健方面投入更多资金。

definition: To fall or move suddenly and uncontrollably downward
exampleSentence: Stock prices plunged dramatically after the negative earnings report.
exampleTranslation: 在负面盈利报告发布后，股价大幅暴跌。

definition: The presence of harmful substances in the natural environment
exampleSentence: Air pollution in major cities has become a serious public health concern.
exampleTranslation: 大城市的空气污染已成为严重的公共健康问题。

definition: Able to be easily carried or moved from place to place
exampleSentence: The portable speaker is perfect for outdoor activities and travel.
exampleTranslation: 这款便携式音箱非常适合户外活动和旅行。

definition: To present a problem or to assume a particular position
exampleSentence: Climate change poses a serious threat to global food security.
exampleTranslation: 气候变化对全球粮食安全构成了严重威胁。

definition: Having the capacity to develop into something in the future
exampleSentence: The new technology has the potential to revolutionize the industry.
exampleTranslation: 新技术有可能彻底改变这个行业。

definition: A measure taken in advance to prevent something undesirable
exampleSentence: Travelers should take precautions against tropical diseases when abroad.
exampleTranslation: 旅行者在国外时应采取预防热带疾病的措施。

definition: To come before something in time, order, or importance
exampleSentence: A brief introduction will precede the main presentation this morning.
exampleTranslation: 今天上午的主要报告之前会有一个简短的介绍。

definition: An earlier event or action that serves as an example
exampleSentence: The court's ruling set an important legal precedent for future cases.
exampleTranslation: 法院的裁决为未来的案件开创了重要的法律先例。

definition: Marked by exactness and accuracy of expression or detail
exampleSentence: The scientist provided a precise measurement of the chemical compound.
exampleTranslation: 科学家提供了化合物的精确测量数据。

definition: Present as the strongest or main element; most common
exampleSentence: Agriculture is the predominant industry in the rural region.
exampleTranslation: 农业是该农村地区的主要产业。

definition: Preconceived opinion not based on reason or experience
exampleSentence: Education is the best tool for combating prejudice and discrimination.
exampleTranslation: 教育是消除偏见和歧视的最好工具。

definition: Preceding the main event or denoting an initial stage
exampleSentence: The preliminary results suggest the treatment is highly effective.
exampleTranslation: 初步结果表明该治疗方法非常有效。

definition: A statement assumed true as basis for argument; a building
exampleSentence: The entire theory is based on the premise that resources are limited.
exampleTranslation: 整个理论建立在资源有限的前提之上。

definition: An amount paid for an insurance policy or something superior
exampleSentence: Customers are willing to pay a premium for organic food products.
exampleTranslation: 顾客愿意为有机食品支付额外费用。

definition: To advise or order the use of medicine or a course of action
exampleSentence: The doctor prescribed antibiotics to treat the bacterial infection.
exampleTranslation: 医生开了抗生素来治疗细菌感染。

definition: Widespread respect and admiration based on achievement
exampleSentence: The university has earned great prestige for its research excellence.
exampleTranslation: 该大学因其卓越的研究而赢得了崇高的声望。

definition: Used to convey that something is very likely the case
exampleSentence: The meeting was cancelled, presumably because the director was ill.
exampleTranslation: 会议被取消了，大概是因为主任生病了。

definition: To prove more powerful than opposing forces; to be widespread
exampleSentence: Justice will ultimately prevail despite the current difficulties.
exampleTranslation: 尽管目前面临困难，正义终将占上风。

definition: Widespread or commonly occurring at a particular time
exampleSentence: Diabetes is increasingly prevalent among younger age groups today.
exampleTranslation: 如今，糖尿病在年轻人群中越来越普遍。

definition: Relating to an early stage of development; basic and simple
exampleSentence: The explorers discovered primitive tools used by ancient humans.
exampleTranslation: 探险家发现了古代人类使用的原始工具。

definition: Existing or coming before in time or importance
exampleSentence: Prior experience in management is required for this senior position.
exampleTranslation: 这个高级职位需要之前的管理经验。

definition: A special right or advantage available only to certain people
exampleSentence: Access to quality education should be a right, not a privilege.
exampleTranslation: 获得优质教育应该是一项权利，而不是特权。

definition: To physically explore or investigate something thoroughly
exampleSentence: The committee decided to probe the allegations of corruption.
exampleTranslation: 委员会决定调查腐败指控。

definition: To begin or continue a course of action after a pause
exampleSentence: After the break we will proceed with the second part of meeting.
exampleTranslation: 休息之后我们将继续会议的第二部分。

definition: To announce officially or publicly with great confidence
exampleSentence: The president proclaimed a national day of mourning for the victims.
exampleTranslation: 总统宣布为遇难者设立全国哀悼日。

definition: Achieving a significant amount of results; effective
exampleSentence: The meeting was highly productive and resulted in several decisions.
exampleTranslation: 会议非常富有成效，做出了几项决定。

definition: Very great or intense; having deep insight and understanding
exampleSentence: The discovery had a profound impact on the field of medical science.
exampleTranslation: 这一发现对医学领域产生了深远的影响。

definition: To formally forbid something by law or authority
exampleSentence: The regulations prohibit smoking in all enclosed public spaces.
exampleTranslation: 法规禁止在所有封闭的公共场所吸烟。

definition: To estimate or forecast based on current data; to extend outward
exampleSentence: Analysts project that the economy will grow by four percent.
exampleTranslation: 分析师预计经济将增长百分之四。

definition: Important and well known; easily noticeable and standing out
exampleSentence: The prominent scientist received the Nobel Prize for his research.
exampleTranslation: 这位著名科学家因其研究获得了诺贝尔奖。

definition: To further the progress of; to raise to a higher position
exampleSentence: The organization works to promote awareness of mental health issues.
exampleTranslation: 该组织致力于提高人们对心理健康问题的认识。

definition: Done without delay; causing or encouraging someone to act
exampleSentence: Prompt action by the emergency services saved many lives.
exampleTranslation: 紧急服务部门的迅速行动挽救了许多生命。

definition: Likely or liable to suffer from or do something undesirable
exampleSentence: The coastal area is prone to flooding during the monsoon season.
exampleTranslation: 沿海地区在季风季节容易发生洪水。

definition: Information used to promote a particular political cause
exampleSentence: The government used propaganda to influence public opinion during war.
exampleTranslation: 政府在战争期间使用宣传来影响公众舆论。

definition: A part or share of a whole; the relationship between amounts
exampleSentence: A large proportion of the budget is allocated to national defense.
exampleTranslation: 预算的很大一部分被分配给了国防。

definition: The possibility of future success or a likely candidate
exampleSentence: The prospect of economic recovery has improved in recent months.
exampleTranslation: 近几个月来经济复苏的前景有所改善。

definition: The state of being successful and having good fortune
exampleSentence: The country enjoyed a long period of peace and economic prosperity.
exampleTranslation: 该国享有长期的和平与经济繁荣。

definition: The official procedure or system of rules governing affairs
exampleSentence: Safety protocol requires all workers to wear protective equipment.
exampleTranslation: 安全协议要求所有工人穿戴防护装备。

definition: To stimulate or give rise to a reaction or strong feeling
exampleSentence: The controversial speech provoked widespread protests across the nation.
exampleTranslation: 这篇有争议的演讲引发了全国范围的广泛抗议。

definition: Acting with care and thought for the future; wise
exampleSentence: It would be prudent to save money before making large investments.
exampleTranslation: 在进行大额投资之前存钱是谨慎的做法。

definition: To make something widely known to the general public
exampleSentence: The company hired a firm to publicize its new product launch.
exampleTranslation: 公司聘请了一家公司来宣传其新产品发布。

definition: Happening or doing something at the agreed or proper time
exampleSentence: Japanese trains are famous for being extremely punctual and reliable.
exampleTranslation: 日本列车以极其准时和可靠而闻名。

definition: To buy something in exchange for payment of money
exampleSentence: The company plans to purchase new equipment for the laboratory.
exampleTranslation: 公司计划为实验室购买新设备。

definition: To follow or chase someone or to continue with an activity
exampleSentence: She decided to pursue a career in international law after graduation.
exampleTranslation: 她决定毕业后从事国际法方面的职业。

definition: A skill or accomplishment that makes someone suitable
exampleSentence: A master's degree is the minimum qualification for this research role.
exampleTranslation: 硕士学位是这个研究职位的最低资格要求。

definition: To meet the conditions required to receive or achieve something
exampleSentence: You must pass the exam to qualify for the professional certificate.
exampleTranslation: 你必须通过考试才能获得职业证书的资格。

definition: To express or measure the quantity of something numerically
exampleSentence: It is difficult to quantify the full economic impact of the crisis.
exampleTranslation: 很难量化这场危机的全部经济影响。

definition: A long search for something important or a pursuit of a goal
exampleSentence: The scientist devoted her life to the quest for a cancer cure.
exampleTranslation: 这位科学家毕生致力于寻找癌症的治疗方法。

definition: To repeat the exact words someone has written or spoken
exampleSentence: The article quotes several experts on the topic of climate change.
exampleTranslation: 文章引用了几位关于气候变化话题的专家的话。

definition: Relating to or based on a person's race or ethnic origin
exampleSentence: The organization promotes racial equality and social justice.
exampleTranslation: 该组织倡导种族平等和社会正义。

definition: Relating to fundamental change; extreme or thoroughgoing
exampleSentence: The government introduced radical reforms to modernize the economy.
exampleTranslation: 政府引入了激进的改革以实现经济现代化。

definition: Made or happening without method or conscious decision
exampleSentence: The survey selected participants at random from the general population.
exampleTranslation: 调查从普通人群中随机选取了参与者。

definition: The scope or extent of something; a set of different things
exampleSentence: The store offers a wide range of products for every budget.
exampleTranslation: 这家商店提供适合各种预算的多种产品。

definition: To give formal consent to a treaty or agreement officially
exampleSentence: All member states must ratify the treaty for it to take effect.
exampleTranslation: 所有成员国必须批准该条约才能使其生效。

definition: Based on or in accordance with reason or logic
exampleSentence: The committee made a rational decision based on the available data.
exampleTranslation: 委员会根据可用数据做出了理性的决定。

definition: Without thinking about the consequences of an action
exampleSentence: Reckless driving is one of the leading causes of traffic accidents.
exampleTranslation: 鲁莽驾驶是交通事故的主要原因之一。

definition: To restore friendly relations or to make things compatible
exampleSentence: The couple tried to reconcile their differences through counseling.
exampleTranslation: 这对夫妇试图通过咨询调和他们的分歧。

definition: To enlist new members or find suitable people for a job
exampleSentence: The company is actively recruiting new engineers for the project.
exampleTranslation: 公司正在积极招聘新工程师参与该项目。

definition: Not needed; exceeding what is necessary or sufficient
exampleSentence: Many factory workers were made redundant after the automation upgrade.
exampleTranslation: 自动化升级后，许多工厂工人被裁员。

definition: To remove impurities or to improve something by making changes
exampleSentence: The team continued to refine the design based on user feedback.
exampleTranslation: 团队根据用户反馈继续改进设计。

definition: The act of making changes to improve a system or institution
exampleSentence: The education reform aims to improve access and quality for all.
exampleTranslation: 教育改革旨在提高所有人的入学机会和教育质量。

definition: To prove a statement or theory to be wrong or false
exampleSentence: The evidence presented was strong enough to refute the original claim.
exampleTranslation: 所提供的证据足以反驳最初的说法。

definition: A system of government or a set of rules for management
exampleSentence: The authoritarian regime suppressed freedom of speech and press.
exampleTranslation: 独裁政权压制了言论和新闻自由。

definition: To control or maintain the rate or speed of something
exampleSentence: The government introduced new laws to regulate the banking industry.
exampleTranslation: 政府出台了新法律来监管银行业。

definition: To strengthen or support something with additional resources
exampleSentence: The company plans to reinforce its cybersecurity measures this year.
exampleTranslation: 公司计划今年加强网络安全措施。

definition: Closely connected or appropriate to the matter at hand
exampleSentence: The applicant must have relevant experience in the technology sector.
exampleTranslation: 申请人必须具有技术行业的相关经验。

definition: Consistently good in quality or performance; dependable
